## Change (3 files, additive/low-risk)

  primitive) + `--shadow-card` / `--shadow-card-hover` (brand-temperature elevation using
  the locked #c8102e=rgb(200,16,46) — neutral hairline + faint red wash, NOT a palette
  change). Purely additive (new utilities `shadow-card`, `ease-standard`).
- `components/ui/card.tsx`: add `shadow-card` + `transition-shadow duration-150 ease-standard`
  → restrained premium elevation on every Card product-wide (was ring-only/flat).
- `docs/web/s_tier_design_system.md`: canonical S-tier reference.

## Acceptance
- AC1: additive tokens; no existing utility/token value changed; brand red untouched.
- AC2: Card gains brand-tinted elevation + smooth shadow transition; no layout shift.
- AC3: build + eslint + prettier green.

LIFT (expensive temperature) not a regression. NOTE: visual_60 baselines will need refresh
(intentional global shadow change).

:root {
  /* I-ui-010 (#730): Frontier Minimal — near-monochrome zinc + ONE confident
     accent (default = blue oklch(0.50 0.18 255); the prior cyan read grey).

     oklch(0.50 0.16 270) / teal oklch(0.52 0.12 215)). chart-* left mono. */

  --foreground: oklch(0.21 0.006 285.9);

     red-only): verified=green, contradiction=amber, refusal=neutral-strong,
     destructive=deep maroon distinct from the national red. + evidence-role
     tokens (tiers + proof). Operator design lock 2026-05-21. */

+# POLARIS S-tier design system (canonical)
+
+**Status:** authoritative. Source: Codex vision-authored direction
+(`.codex/ui_visual_audit/s_tier_direction_verdict.txt`, 2026-05-23), grounded in
+2026 best practice + Stripe/Linear/Vercel premium-UI invariants. Bar = **A++/S**,
+visually competitive with AND differentiated from Perplexity / ChatGPT Deep
+Research / Gemini. Tracking issue: #829 (umbrella), foundation #831 (I-p2-042).
+
+## The signature move (the differentiator)
+
+**Proof Replay is the product's visual operating system, not a component.**
+Every screen reuses the same grammar: `claim sentence → provenance token → cited
+source span → evaluator verdict → audit hash`. "Perplexity owns sources-near-answers;
+POLARIS owns _every sentence is cross-examinable_." Proof tokens, the proof rail,
+span highlights, and verdict badges appear consistently across the product.
+
+## Tokens
+
+- **Type:** Geist only. Scale: display 40/44·700, h1 32/38·700, h2 24/30·700,
+  h3 18/26·600, body 16/24·400, body-sm 14/21·400, caption 12/16·500 (uppercase
+  labels +0.08em), mono 13/18·500. Tabular nums for clinical values; mono for
+  IDs/hashes/dates/model names.
+- **Spacing grid:** 4 / 8 / 12 / 16 / 24 / 32 / 48 / 64 / 96. Page top pad 64
+  (desktop) / 32 (mobile); section gap 48; card pad 24; inspector pad 16; form
+  field gap 16. Max content 1120px / workbench 1200px. Spacing must be _invisible
+  because systematic_ — never mix 16 and 24 arbitrarily.
+- **Color (restraint):** near-neutral + ONE accent — **Canada red `#c8102e`
+  (operator-locked 2026-05-21; do NOT change)** used once/screen, meaning-only
+  (primary action or active proof mark, never decoration). Verified = green
+  (`--verified`), warning = amber (`--contradiction`), danger = `--destructive`,
+  refusal = neutral-strong. Evidence-role tokens: tier-1/2/3, proof-token,
+  verified-bundle.
+- **Radius:** cards `rounded-xl` (~12px), controls ~8px, pills full.
+- **Border / shadow:** hairline `ring-foreground/10` (1px low-alpha) plus
+  brand-tinted elevation `--shadow-card` / `--shadow-card-hover` (a neutral
+  hairline lift and a faint Canada-red wash — never pure gray).
+- **Motion:** ONE primitive — `--ease-standard: cubic-bezier(0.2,0.8,0.2,1)`,
+  ~150–160ms. Reuse on hover/focus/tab/inspector-selection/span-reveal/skeleton.
+  No ornamental animation.
+- **Microstates (non-negotiable, every interactive element):** default / hover
+  (brand-soft or neutral tint) / focus (custom 2px ring `ring-ring/…` + 2px
+  offset, visible) / active (slight inset, buttons translateY(1px)) / disabled
+  (opacity ~.45, no pointer) / loading (skeleton matching layout, never a bare
+  spinner).
+- **Empty/loading/error:** evidence-native + designed (ghost timeseries, claim-row
+  skeletons, contract preview shell). Errors explain _which_ gate failed
+  (scope / coverage / evaluator disagreement / infrastructure).
+
+## Verification protocol (every UI PR)
+
+1. GitHub issue first; close on merge.
+2. Build the page/primitive to the spec; preserve logic + `data-testid`s.
+3. **Dual visual gate:** Claude views the screenshots AND Codex audits them via
+   `codex exec -i` at the A++/S bar — every state (empty/loading/error/populated)
+   × desktop/tablet/mobile. Plus Codex CODE review (brief + diff).
+4. e2e (Playwright) green; visual-regression baselines refreshed on intentional
+   pixel changes.
+5. Update this doc + `docs/file_directory.md`; deploy + live-verify.
+
+## Build order
+
+1. **Foundation tokens** (#831 / I-p2-042) — motion + brand-tinted shadow applied
+   to Card; type/spacing/color already tokenized. ← _in progress_
+2. **Primitives** — Button/Input/Select/Chip/Tab/Card/Badge/Skeleton/EmptyState,
+   plus the proof primitives (ProofToken/ProofRail/SourceSpan/VerdictBadge).
+3. **Proof system** — ClaimStack / ProofReplayPanel / EvaluatorVerdict /
+   AuditHashChain.
+4. **Pages** (S-bar, worst-gap weighted): Inspector → Home → Intake → Contracts →
+   Upload → Pin Replay → Sign-in.
+5. **Cred-gated** (need `POLARIS_DEMO_USER`/`POLARIS_DEMO_PASS`): dashboard,
+   benchmark, memory, source-review, and the Plan → Run → Compare journey.
+
+## Per-screen S-bar baseline (Codex, 2026-05-23 — pre-redo)
+
+Inspector B+, Home B, Intake B−, Contracts B− (post first rebuild), Sign-in B−,
+Upload C+, Pin Replay C. Target every screen at A++/S with the signature move
+systematized.

# POLARIS S-tier design system (canonical)

**Status:** authoritative. Source: Codex vision-authored direction
(`.codex/ui_visual_audit/s_tier_direction_verdict.txt`, 2026-05-23), grounded in
2026 best practice + Stripe/Linear/Vercel premium-UI invariants. Bar = **A++/S**,
visually competitive with AND differentiated from Perplexity / ChatGPT Deep
Research / Gemini. Tracking issue: #829 (umbrella), foundation #831 (I-p2-042).

## The signature move (the differentiator)

**Proof Replay is the product's visual operating system, not a component.**
Every screen reuses the same grammar: `claim sentence → provenance token → cited
source span → evaluator verdict → audit hash`. "Perplexity owns sources-near-answers;
POLARIS owns _every sentence is cross-examinable_." Proof tokens, the proof rail,
span highlights, and verdict badges appear consistently across the product.

## Tokens

- **Type:** Geist only. Scale: display 40/44·700, h1 32/38·700, h2 24/30·700,
  h3 18/26·600, body 16/24·400, body-sm 14/21·400, caption 12/16·500 (uppercase
  labels +0.08em), mono 13/18·500. Tabular nums for clinical values; mono for
  IDs/hashes/dates/model names.
- **Spacing grid:** 4 / 8 / 12 / 16 / 24 / 32 / 48 / 64 / 96. Page top pad 64
  (desktop) / 32 (mobile); section gap 48; card pad 24; inspector pad 16; form
  field gap 16. Max content 1120px / workbench 1200px. Spacing must be _invisible
  because systematic_ — never mix 16 and 24 arbitrarily.
- **Color (restraint):** near-neutral + ONE accent — **Canada red `#c8102e`
  (operator-locked 2026-05-21; do NOT change)** used once/screen, meaning-only
  (primary action or active proof mark, never decoration). Verified = green
  (`--verified`), warning = amber (`--contradiction`), danger = `--destructive`,
  refusal = neutral-strong. Evidence-role tokens: tier-1/2/3, proof-token,
  verified-bundle.
- **Radius:** cards `rounded-xl` (~12px), controls ~8px, pills full.
- **Border / shadow:** hairline `ring-foreground/10` (1px low-alpha) plus
  brand-tinted elevation `--shadow-card` / `--shadow-card-hover` (a neutral
  hairline lift and a faint Canada-red wash — never pure gray).
- **Motion:** ONE primitive — `--ease-standard: cubic-bezier(0.2,0.8,0.2,1)`,
  ~150–160ms. Reuse on hover/focus/tab/inspector-selection/span-reveal/skeleton.
  No ornamental animation.
- **Microstates (non-negotiable, every interactive element):** default / hover
  (brand-soft or neutral tint) / focus (custom 2px ring `ring-ring/…` + 2px
  offset, visible) / active (slight inset, buttons translateY(1px)) / disabled
  (opacity ~.45, no pointer) / loading (skeleton matching layout, never a bare
  spinner).
- **Empty/loading/error:** evidence-native + designed (ghost timeseries, claim-row
  skeletons, contract preview shell). Errors explain _which_ gate failed
  (scope / coverage / evaluator disagreement / infrastructure).

## Verification protocol (every UI PR)

1. GitHub issue first; close on merge.
2. Build the page/primitive to the spec; preserve logic + `data-testid`s.
3. **Dual visual gate:** Claude views the screenshots AND Codex audits them via
   `codex exec -i` at the A++/S bar — every state (empty/loading/error/populated)
   × desktop/tablet/mobile. Plus Codex CODE review (brief + diff).
4. e2e (Playwright) green; visual-regression baselines refreshed on intentional
   pixel changes.
5. Update this doc + `docs/file_directory.md`; deploy + live-verify.

## Build order

1. **Foundation tokens** (#831 / I-p2-042) — motion + brand-tinted shadow applied
   to Card; type/spacing/color already tokenized. ← _in progress_
2. **Primitives** — Button/Input/Select/Chip/Tab/Card/Badge/Skeleton/EmptyState,
   plus the proof primitives (ProofToken/ProofRail/SourceSpan/VerdictBadge).
3. **Proof system** — ClaimStack / ProofReplayPanel / EvaluatorVerdict /
   AuditHashChain.
4. **Pages** (S-bar, worst-gap weighted): Inspector → Home → Intake → Contracts →
   Upload → Pin Replay → Sign-in.
5. **Cred-gated** (need `POLARIS_DEMO_USER`/`POLARIS_DEMO_PASS`): dashboard,
   benchmark, memory, source-review, and the Plan → Run → Compare journey.

## Per-screen S-bar baseline (Codex, 2026-05-23 — pre-redo)

Inspector B+, Home B, Intake B−, Contracts B− (post first rebuild), Sign-in B−,
Upload C+, Pin Replay C. Target every screen at A++/S with the signature move
systematized.

import * as React from "react";

import { cn } from "@/lib/utils";

function Card({
  className,
  size = "default",
  ...props
}: React.ComponentProps<"div"> & { size?: "default" | "sm" }) {
  return (
    <div
      data-slot="card"
      data-size={size}
      className={cn(
        // I-p2-042 (#831) S-tier foundation: brand-tinted card elevation
        // (shadow-card) + the shared motion primitive on shadow transitions.
        "group/card bg-card text-card-foreground ring-foreground/10 shadow-card ease-standard flex flex-col gap-4 overflow-hidden rounded-xl py-4 text-sm ring-1 transition-shadow duration-150 has-data-[slot=card-footer]:pb-0 has-[>img:first-child]:pt-0 data-[size=sm]:gap-3 data-[size=sm]:py-3 data-[size=sm]:has-data-[slot=card-footer]:pb-0 *:[img:first-child]:rounded-t-xl *:[img:last-child]:rounded-b-xl",
        className,
      )}
      {...props}
    />
  );
}

function CardHeader({ className, ...props }: React.ComponentProps<"div">) {
  return (
    <div
      data-slot="card-header"
      className={cn(
        "group/card-header @container/card-header grid auto-rows-min items-start gap-1 rounded-t-xl px-4 group-data-[size=sm]/card:px-3 has-data-[slot=card-action]:grid-cols-[1fr_auto] has-data-[slot=card-description]:grid-rows-[auto_auto] [.border-b]:pb-4 group-data-[size=sm]/card:[.border-b]:pb-3",
        className,
      )}
      {...props}
    />
  );
}

function CardTitle({ className, ...props }: React.ComponentProps<"div">) {
  return (
    <div
      data-slot="card-title"
      className={cn(
        "font-heading text-base leading-snug font-medium group-data-[size=sm]/card:text-sm",
        className,
      )}
      {...props}
    />
  );
}

function CardDescription({ className, ...props }: React.ComponentProps<"div">) {
  return (
    <div
      data-slot="card-description"
      className={cn("text-muted-foreground text-sm", className)}
      {...props}
    />
  );
}

function CardAction({ className, ...props }: React.ComponentProps<"div">) {
  return (
    <div
      data-slot="card-action"
      className={cn(
        "col-start-2 row-span-2 row-start-1 self-start justify-self-end",
        className,
      )}
      {...props}
    />
  );
}

function CardContent({ className, ...props }: React.ComponentProps<"div">) {
  return (
    <div
      data-slot="card-content"
      className={cn("px-4 group-data-[size=sm]/card:px-3", className)}
      {...props}
    />
  );
}

function CardFooter({ className, ...props }: React.ComponentProps<"div">) {
  return (
    <div
      data-slot="card-footer"
      className={cn(
        "bg-muted/50 flex items-center rounded-b-xl border-t p-4 group-data-[size=sm]/card:p-3",
        className,
      )}
      {...props}
    />
  );
}

export {
  Card,
  CardHeader,
  CardFooter,
  CardTitle,
  CardAction,
  CardDescription,
  CardContent,
};

one-off validation drivers, dispatcher/serve variants, auto-pilot
helpers, etc. These survived the Phase A archive because they contain
`if __name__ == "__main__":` (making them "reachable" by my
static-analysis criterion). Candidates for future cleanup:

 ## 10. Deprecated references you may encounter
 
 The following appear in some remaining scripts or old doc strings but
 point to non-existent paths / deprecated concepts. Treat as stale:
 
 - `src/phases/` — the P0-P12 directory structure. Removed before
   2026-04-18. The README's old "13-phase pipeline" description was
   fiction as of this cleanup.
 - `src/runner.py` — referenced in old README, does not exist.
 - `scripts/preflight.py`, `scripts/flight_test.py`,
   `scripts/postflight_audit.py` — referenced in old README, do not
   exist. Use `scripts/pg_preflight.py` instead.
 - `scripts/final_audit.py`, `scripts/run_ragas_v3.py` — referenced
   by `scripts/full_cycle.py` (pipeline C). Do not exist; pipeline C
   is broken until these are either restored or removed.
 - "Kimi K2.5 1T" — historical generator. The locked generator/evaluator
   for the Carney demo is DeepSeek V4 Pro + Gemma 4 31B (see
   `docs/polaris_locked_scope.md` §1); earlier pipelines used DeepSeek
   V3.2-Exp + Qwen3-8B.
 - "175 vectors exactly" — old invariant from P0-P12. Not applicable
   to any currently-active pipeline.
 
 See `archive/2026-04-18-pre-audit-cleanup/docs/architecture_legacy_2026-01-31.md`

 # POLARIS S-tier design system (canonical)
 
 **Status:** authoritative. Source: Codex vision-authored direction
 (`.codex/ui_visual_audit/s_tier_direction_verdict.txt`, 2026-05-23), grounded in
 2026 best practice + Stripe/Linear/Vercel premium-UI invariants. Bar = **A++/S**,
 visually competitive with AND differentiated from Perplexity / ChatGPT Deep
 Research / Gemini. Tracking issue: #829 (umbrella), foundation #831 (I-p2-042).
 
 ## The signature move (the differentiator)
+
 **Proof Replay is the product's visual operating system, not a component.**
 Every screen reuses the same grammar: `claim sentence → provenance token → cited
 source span → evaluator verdict → audit hash`. "Perplexity owns sources-near-answers;
-POLARIS owns *every sentence is cross-examinable*." Proof tokens, the proof rail,
+POLARIS owns _every sentence is cross-examinable_." Proof tokens, the proof rail,
 span highlights, and verdict badges appear consistently across the product.
 
 ## Tokens
+
 - **Type:** Geist only. Scale: display 40/44·700, h1 32/38·700, h2 24/30·700,
   h3 18/26·600, body 16/24·400, body-sm 14/21·400, caption 12/16·500 (uppercase
   labels +0.08em), mono 13/18·500. Tabular nums for clinical values; mono for
   IDs/hashes/dates/model names.
 - **Spacing grid:** 4 / 8 / 12 / 16 / 24 / 32 / 48 / 64 / 96. Page top pad 64
   (desktop) / 32 (mobile); section gap 48; card pad 24; inspector pad 16; form
-  field gap 16. Max content 1120px / workbench 1200px. Spacing must be *invisible
-  because systematic* — never mix 16 and 24 arbitrarily.
+  field gap 16. Max content 1120px / workbench 1200px. Spacing must be _invisible
+  because systematic_ — never mix 16 and 24 arbitrarily.
 - **Color (restraint):** near-neutral + ONE accent — **Canada red `#c8102e`
   (operator-locked 2026-05-21; do NOT change)** used once/screen, meaning-only
   (primary action or active proof mark, never decoration). Verified = green
   (`--verified`), warning = amber (`--contradiction`), danger = `--destructive`,
   refusal = neutral-strong. Evidence-role tokens: tier-1/2/3, proof-token,
   verified-bundle.
 - **Radius:** cards `rounded-xl` (~12px), controls ~8px, pills full.
-- **Border / shadow:** hairline `ring-foreground/10` (1px low-alpha) +
-  brand-tinted elevation `--shadow-card` / `--shadow-card-hover` (neutral hairline
-  + faint Canada-red wash — never pure gray).
+- **Border / shadow:** hairline `ring-foreground/10` (1px low-alpha) plus
+  brand-tinted elevation `--shadow-card` / `--shadow-card-hover` (a neutral
+  hairline lift and a faint Canada-red wash — never pure gray).
 - **Motion:** ONE primitive — `--ease-standard: cubic-bezier(0.2,0.8,0.2,1)`,
   ~150–160ms. Reuse on hover/focus/tab/inspector-selection/span-reveal/skeleton.
   No ornamental animation.
 - **Microstates (non-negotiable, every interactive element):** default / hover
   (brand-soft or neutral tint) / focus (custom 2px ring `ring-ring/…` + 2px
   offset, visible) / active (slight inset, buttons translateY(1px)) / disabled
   (opacity ~.45, no pointer) / loading (skeleton matching layout, never a bare
   spinner).
 - **Empty/loading/error:** evidence-native + designed (ghost timeseries, claim-row
-  skeletons, contract preview shell). Errors explain *which* gate failed
+  skeletons, contract preview shell). Errors explain _which_ gate failed
   (scope / coverage / evaluator disagreement / infrastructure).
 
 ## Verification protocol (every UI PR)
+
 1. GitHub issue first; close on merge.
 2. Build the page/primitive to the spec; preserve logic + `data-testid`s.
 3. **Dual visual gate:** Claude views the screenshots AND Codex audits them via
    `codex exec -i` at the A++/S bar — every state (empty/loading/error/populated)
    × desktop/tablet/mobile. Plus Codex CODE review (brief + diff).
 4. e2e (Playwright) green; visual-regression baselines refreshed on intentional
    pixel changes.
 5. Update this doc + `docs/file_directory.md`; deploy + live-verify.
 
 ## Build order
+
 1. **Foundation tokens** (#831 / I-p2-042) — motion + brand-tinted shadow applied
-   to Card; type/spacing/color already tokenized. ← *in progress*
-2. **Primitives** — Button/Input/Select/Chip/Tab/Card/Badge/Skeleton/EmptyState
-   + proof primitives (ProofToken/ProofRail/SourceSpan/VerdictBadge).
+   to Card; type/spacing/color already tokenized. ← _in progress_
+2. **Primitives** — Button/Input/Select/Chip/Tab/Card/Badge/Skeleton/EmptyState,
+   plus the proof primitives (ProofToken/ProofRail/SourceSpan/VerdictBadge).
 3. **Proof system** — ClaimStack / ProofReplayPanel / EvaluatorVerdict /
    AuditHashChain.
 4. **Pages** (S-bar, worst-gap weighted): Inspector → Home → Intake → Contracts →
    Upload → Pin Replay → Sign-in.
 5. **Cred-gated** (need `POLARIS_DEMO_USER`/`POLARIS_DEMO_PASS`): dashboard,
    benchmark, memory, source-review, and the Plan → Run → Compare journey.

+# POLARIS S-tier design system (canonical)
+
+**Status:** authoritative. Source: Codex vision-authored direction
+(`.codex/ui_visual_audit/s_tier_direction_verdict.txt`, 2026-05-23), grounded in
+2026 best practice + Stripe/Linear/Vercel premium-UI invariants. Bar = **A++/S**,
+visually competitive with AND differentiated from Perplexity / ChatGPT Deep
+Research / Gemini. Tracking issue: #829 (umbrella), foundation #831 (I-p2-042).
+
+## The signature move (the differentiator)
+**Proof Replay is the product's visual operating system, not a component.**
+Every screen reuses the same grammar: `claim sentence → provenance token → cited
+source span → evaluator verdict → audit hash`. "Perplexity owns sources-near-answers;
+POLARIS owns *every sentence is cross-examinable*." Proof tokens, the proof rail,
+span highlights, and verdict badges appear consistently across the product.
+
+## Tokens
+- **Type:** Geist only. Scale: display 40/44·700, h1 32/38·700, h2 24/30·700,
+  h3 18/26·600, body 16/24·400, body-sm 14/21·400, caption 12/16·500 (uppercase
+  labels +0.08em), mono 13/18·500. Tabular nums for clinical values; mono for
+  IDs/hashes/dates/model names.
+- **Spacing grid:** 4 / 8 / 12 / 16 / 24 / 32 / 48 / 64 / 96. Page top pad 64
+  (desktop) / 32 (mobile); section gap 48; card pad 24; inspector pad 16; form
+  field gap 16. Max content 1120px / workbench 1200px. Spacing must be *invisible
+  because systematic* — never mix 16 and 24 arbitrarily.
+- **Color (restraint):** near-neutral + ONE accent — **Canada red `#c8102e`
+  (operator-locked 2026-05-21; do NOT change)** used once/screen, meaning-only
+  (primary action or active proof mark, never decoration). Verified = green
+  (`--verified`), warning = amber (`--contradiction`), danger = `--destructive`,
+  refusal = neutral-strong. Evidence-role tokens: tier-1/2/3, proof-token,
+  verified-bundle.
+- **Radius:** cards `rounded-xl` (~12px), controls ~8px, pills full.
+- **Border / shadow:** hairline `ring-foreground/10` (1px low-alpha) +
+  brand-tinted elevation `--shadow-card` / `--shadow-card-hover` (neutral hairline
+  + faint Canada-red wash — never pure gray).
+- **Motion:** ONE primitive — `--ease-standard: cubic-bezier(0.2,0.8,0.2,1)`,
+  ~150–160ms. Reuse on hover/focus/tab/inspector-selection/span-reveal/skeleton.
+  No ornamental animation.
+- **Microstates (non-negotiable, every interactive element):** default / hover
+  (brand-soft or neutral tint) / focus (custom 2px ring `ring-ring/…` + 2px
+  offset, visible) / active (slight inset, buttons translateY(1px)) / disabled
+  (opacity ~.45, no pointer) / loading (skeleton matching layout, never a bare
+  spinner).
+- **Empty/loading/error:** evidence-native + designed (ghost timeseries, claim-row
+  skeletons, contract preview shell). Errors explain *which* gate failed
+  (scope / coverage / evaluator disagreement / infrastructure).
+
+## Verification protocol (every UI PR)
+1. GitHub issue first; close on merge.
+2. Build the page/primitive to the spec; preserve logic + `data-testid`s.
+3. **Dual visual gate:** Claude views the screenshots AND Codex audits them via
+   `codex exec -i` at the A++/S bar — every state (empty/loading/error/populated)
+   × desktop/tablet/mobile. Plus Codex CODE review (brief + diff).
+4. e2e (Playwright) green; visual-regression baselines refreshed on intentional
+   pixel changes.
+5. Update this doc + `docs/file_directory.md`; deploy + live-verify.
+
+## Build order
+1. **Foundation tokens** (#831 / I-p2-042) — motion + brand-tinted shadow applied
+   to Card; type/spacing/color already tokenized. ← *in progress*
+2. **Primitives** — Button/Input/Select/Chip/Tab/Card/Badge/Skeleton/EmptyState
+   + proof primitives (ProofToken/ProofRail/SourceSpan/VerdictBadge).
+3. **Proof system** — ClaimStack / ProofReplayPanel / EvaluatorVerdict /
+   AuditHashChain.
+4. **Pages** (S-bar, worst-gap weighted): Inspector → Home → Intake → Contracts →
+   Upload → Pin Replay → Sign-in.
+5. **Cred-gated** (need `POLARIS_DEMO_USER`/`POLARIS_DEMO_PASS`): dashboard,
+   benchmark, memory, source-review, and the Plan → Run → Compare journey.
+
+## Per-screen S-bar baseline (Codex, 2026-05-23 — pre-redo)
+Inspector B+, Home B, Intake B−, Contracts B− (post first rebuild), Sign-in B−,
+Upload C+, Pin Replay C. Target every screen at A++/S with the signature move
+systematized.

 import * as React from "react";
 
 import { cn } from "@/lib/utils";
 
 function Card({
   className,
   size = "default",
   ...props
 }: React.ComponentProps<"div"> & { size?: "default" | "sm" }) {
   return (
     <div
       data-slot="card"
       data-size={size}
       className={cn(
-        "group/card bg-card text-card-foreground ring-foreground/10 flex flex-col gap-4 overflow-hidden rounded-xl py-4 text-sm ring-1 has-data-[slot=card-footer]:pb-0 has-[>img:first-child]:pt-0 data-[size=sm]:gap-3 data-[size=sm]:py-3 data-[size=sm]:has-data-[slot=card-footer]:pb-0 *:[img:first-child]:rounded-t-xl *:[img:last-child]:rounded-b-xl",
+        // I-p2-042 (#831) S-tier foundation: brand-tinted card elevation
+        // (shadow-card) + the shared motion primitive on shadow transitions.
+        "group/card bg-card text-card-foreground ring-foreground/10 shadow-card ease-standard flex flex-col gap-4 overflow-hidden rounded-xl py-4 text-sm ring-1 transition-shadow duration-150 has-data-[slot=card-footer]:pb-0 has-[>img:first-child]:pt-0 data-[size=sm]:gap-3 data-[size=sm]:py-3 data-[size=sm]:has-data-[slot=card-footer]:pb-0 *:[img:first-child]:rounded-t-xl *:[img:last-child]:rounded-b-xl",
         className,
       )}
       {...props}
     />
   );
 }
 
 function CardHeader({ className, ...props }: React.ComponentProps<"div">) {
   return (
     <div
       data-slot="card-header"
       className={cn(
         "group/card-header @container/card-header grid auto-rows-min items-start gap-1 rounded-t-xl px-4 group-data-[size=sm]/card:px-3 has-data-[slot=card-action]:grid-cols-[1fr_auto] has-data-[slot=card-description]:grid-rows-[auto_auto] [.border-b]:pb-4 group-data-[size=sm]/card:[.border-b]:pb-3",
         className,
       )}
       {...props}
     />
   );
 }
 
 function CardTitle({ className, ...props }: React.ComponentProps<"div">) {
   return (
     <div
       data-slot="card-title"
       className={cn(
         "font-heading text-base leading-snug font-medium group-data-[size=sm]/card:text-sm",
         className,
       )}
       {...props}
     />
   );
 }
 
 function CardDescription({ className, ...props }: React.ComponentProps<"div">) {
   return (
     <div
       data-slot="card-description"
       className={cn("text-muted-foreground text-sm", className)}
       {...props}
     />
   );
 }
 
 function CardAction({ className, ...props }: React.ComponentProps<"div">) {
   return (
     <div
       data-slot="card-action"
       className={cn(
         "col-start-2 row-span-2 row-start-1 self-start justify-self-end",
         className,
       )}
       {...props}
     />
   );
 }
 
 function CardContent({ className, ...props }: React.ComponentProps<"div">) {
   return (
     <div
       data-slot="card-content"
       className={cn("px-4 group-data-[size=sm]/card:px-3", className)}
       {...props}
     />
   );
 }
 
 function CardFooter({ className, ...props }: React.ComponentProps<"div">) {
   return (
     <div
       data-slot="card-footer"
       className={cn(
         "bg-muted/50 flex items-center rounded-b-xl border-t p-4 group-data-[size=sm]/card:p-3",
         className,
       )}
       {...props}
     />
   );
 }

+# POLARIS S-tier design system (canonical)
+
+**Status:** authoritative. Source: Codex vision-authored direction
+(`.codex/ui_visual_audit/s_tier_direction_verdict.txt`, 2026-05-23), grounded in
+2026 best practice + Stripe/Linear/Vercel premium-UI invariants. Bar = **A++/S**,
+visually competitive with AND differentiated from Perplexity / ChatGPT Deep
+Research / Gemini. Tracking issue: #829 (umbrella), foundation #831 (I-p2-042).
+
+## The signature move (the differentiator)
+
+**Proof Replay is the product's visual operating system, not a component.**
+Every screen reuses the same grammar: `claim sentence → provenance token → cited
+source span → evaluator verdict → audit hash`. "Perplexity owns sources-near-answers;
+POLARIS owns _every sentence is cross-examinable_." Proof tokens, the proof rail,
+span highlights, and verdict badges appear consistently across the product.
+
+## Tokens
+
+- **Type:** Geist only. Scale: display 40/44·700, h1 32/38·700, h2 24/30·700,
+  h3 18/26·600, body 16/24·400, body-sm 14/21·400, caption 12/16·500 (uppercase
+  labels +0.08em), mono 13/18·500. Tabular nums for clinical values; mono for
+  IDs/hashes/dates/model names.
+- **Spacing grid:** 4 / 8 / 12 / 16 / 24 / 32 / 48 / 64 / 96. Page top pad 64
+  (desktop) / 32 (mobile); section gap 48; card pad 24; inspector pad 16; form
+  field gap 16. Max content 1120px / workbench 1200px. Spacing must be _invisible
+  because systematic_ — never mix 16 and 24 arbitrarily.
+- **Color (restraint):** near-neutral + ONE accent — **Canada red `#c8102e`
+  (operator-locked 2026-05-21; do NOT change)** used once/screen, meaning-only
+  (primary action or active proof mark, never decoration). Verified = green
+  (`--verified`), warning = amber (`--contradiction`), danger = `--destructive`,
+  refusal = neutral-strong. Evidence-role tokens: tier-1/2/3, proof-token,
+  verified-bundle.
+- **Radius:** cards `rounded-xl` (~12px), controls ~8px, pills full.
+- **Border / shadow:** hairline `ring-foreground/10` (1px low-alpha) plus
+  brand-tinted elevation `--shadow-card` / `--shadow-card-hover` (a neutral
+  hairline lift and a faint Canada-red wash — never pure gray).
+- **Motion:** ONE primitive — `--ease-standard: cubic-bezier(0.2,0.8,0.2,1)`,
+  ~150–160ms. Reuse on hover/focus/tab/inspector-selection/span-reveal/skeleton.
+  No ornamental animation.
+- **Microstates (non-negotiable, every interactive element):** default / hover
+  (brand-soft or neutral tint) / focus (custom 2px ring `ring-ring/…` + 2px
+  offset, visible) / active (slight inset, buttons translateY(1px)) / disabled
+  (opacity ~.45, no pointer) / loading (skeleton matching layout, never a bare
+  spinner).
+- **Empty/loading/error:** evidence-native + designed (ghost timeseries, claim-row
+  skeletons, contract preview shell). Errors explain _which_ gate failed
+  (scope / coverage / evaluator disagreement / infrastructure).
+
+## Verification protocol (every UI PR)
+
+1. GitHub issue first; close on merge.
+2. Build the page/primitive to the spec; preserve logic + `data-testid`s.
+3. **Dual visual gate:** Claude views the screenshots AND Codex audits them via
+   `codex exec -i` at the A++/S bar — every state (empty/loading/error/populated)
+   × desktop/tablet/mobile. Plus Codex CODE review (brief + diff).
+4. e2e (Playwright) green; visual-regression baselines refreshed on intentional
+   pixel changes.
+5. Update this doc + `docs/file_directory.md`; deploy + live-verify.
+
+## Build order
+
+1. **Foundation tokens** (#831 / I-p2-042) — motion + brand-tinted shadow applied
+   to Card; type/spacing/color already tokenized. ← _in progress_
+2. **Primitives** — Button/Input/Select/Chip/Tab/Card/Badge/Skeleton/EmptyState,
+   plus the proof primitives (ProofToken/ProofRail/SourceSpan/VerdictBadge).
+3. **Proof system** — ClaimStack / ProofReplayPanel / EvaluatorVerdict /
+   AuditHashChain.
+4. **Pages** (S-bar, worst-gap weighted): Inspector → Home → Intake → Contracts →
+   Upload → Pin Replay → Sign-in.
+5. **Cred-gated** (need `POLARIS_DEMO_USER`/`POLARIS_DEMO_PASS`): dashboard,
+   benchmark, memory, source-review, and the Plan → Run → Compare journey.
+
+## Per-screen S-bar baseline (Codex, 2026-05-23 — pre-redo)
+
+Inspector B+, Home B, Intake B−, Contracts B− (post first rebuild), Sign-in B−,
+Upload C+, Pin Replay C. Target every screen at A++/S with the signature move
+systematized.

+  /* I-p2-042 (#831) S-tier foundation: ONE motion primitive (Codex direction)
+     — consistent inspection/selection feel; reuse on hover/focus/tab/span. */

   /* I-ui-010 (#730): Frontier Minimal — near-monochrome zinc + ONE confident
      accent (default = blue oklch(0.50 0.18 255); the prior cyan read grey).
      AA-measured: primary/white-fg ~4.6:1; accent/accent-fg AA. Hairline
      border. Accent hue is operator-confirmable (one-token swap; alt indigo
      oklch(0.50 0.16 270) / teal oklch(0.52 0.12 215)). chart-* left mono. */

   /* I-p2-003 (#742): dark-mode accent = Canada-red (NOT the old cyan, which
      violated the operator lock). Lighter L0.62 for contrast on the dark
      surface; white fg. NOTE: light mode is the demo target; dark mode reuses

 import * as React from "react";
 
 import { cn } from "@/lib/utils";
 
 function Card({
   className,
   size = "default",
   ...props
 }: React.ComponentProps<"div"> & { size?: "default" | "sm" }) {
   return (
     <div
       data-slot="card"
       data-size={size}
       className={cn(
-        "group/card bg-card text-card-foreground ring-foreground/10 flex flex-col gap-4 overflow-hidden rounded-xl py-4 text-sm ring-1 has-data-[slot=card-footer]:pb-0 has-[>img:first-child]:pt-0 data-[size=sm]:gap-3 data-[size=sm]:py-3 data-[size=sm]:has-data-[slot=card-footer]:pb-0 *:[img:first-child]:rounded-t-xl *:[img:last-child]:rounded-b-xl",
+        // I-p2-042 (#831) S-tier foundation: brand-tinted card elevation
+        // (shadow-card) + the shared motion primitive on shadow transitions.
+        "group/card bg-card text-card-foreground ring-foreground/10 shadow-card ease-standard flex flex-col gap-4 overflow-hidden rounded-xl py-4 text-sm ring-1 transition-shadow duration-150 has-data-[slot=card-footer]:pb-0 has-[>img:first-child]:pt-0 data-[size=sm]:gap-3 data-[size=sm]:py-3 data-[size=sm]:has-data-[slot=card-footer]:pb-0 *:[img:first-child]:rounded-t-xl *:[img:last-child]:rounded-b-xl",
         className,
       )}
       {...props}
     />
   );
 }
 
 function CardHeader({ className, ...props }: React.ComponentProps<"div">) {
   return (
     <div
       data-slot="card-header"
       className={cn(
         "group/card-header @container/card-header grid auto-rows-min items-start gap-1 rounded-t-xl px-4 group-data-[size=sm]/card:px-3 has-data-[slot=card-action]:grid-cols-[1fr_auto] has-data-[slot=card-description]:grid-rows-[auto_auto] [.border-b]:pb-4 group-data-[size=sm]/card:[.border-b]:pb-3",
         className,
       )}
       {...props}
     />
   );
 }
 
 function CardTitle({ className, ...props }: React.ComponentProps<"div">) {
   return (
     <div
       data-slot="card-title"
       className={cn(
         "font-heading text-base leading-snug font-medium group-data-[size=sm]/card:text-sm",
         className,
       )}
       {...props}
     />
   );
 }
 
 function CardDescription({ className, ...props }: React.ComponentProps<"div">) {
   return (
     <div
       data-slot="card-description"
       className={cn("text-muted-foreground text-sm", className)}
       {...props}
     />
   );
 }
 
 function CardAction({ className, ...props }: React.ComponentProps<"div">) {
   return (
     <div
       data-slot="card-action"
       className={cn(
         "col-start-2 row-span-2 row-start-1 self-start justify-self-end",
         className,
       )}
       {...props}
     />
   );
 }
 
 function CardContent({ className, ...props }: React.ComponentProps<"div">) {
   return (
     <div
       data-slot="card-content"
       className={cn("px-4 group-data-[size=sm]/card:px-3", className)}
       {...props}
     />
   );
 }
 
 function CardFooter({ className, ...props }: React.ComponentProps<"div">) {
   return (
     <div
       data-slot="card-footer"
       className={cn(
         "bg-muted/50 flex items-center rounded-b-xl border-t p-4 group-data-[size=sm]/card:p-3",
         className,
       )}
       {...props}
     />
   );
 }

This is the COMPLIANCE/EXPORT artifact: the\r\n// signed-package integrity manifest (file hashes), the pipeline gate ledger,\r\n// the two-family provenance, and the download. Server component — every field\r\n// comes from the proven `loadBundle` path (same loader the inspector + the\r\n// /api/inspector_bundle/[runId] route use); no client JS needed (download is a\r\n// plain <a download> to that route).\r\n//\r\n// Honesty (LAW II): the canonical bundle ships WITHOUT manifest.yaml.asc (the\r\n// GPG seal was deliberately deferred — operator \"skip the seal, build the real\r\n// proof\"). So `signaturePresent` is false and the page says exactly that:\r\n// integrity manifest present, GPG signature pending sovereign signing. It never\r\n// claims a signature that isn't on disk.\r\n//\r\n// The richer per-gate audit log (corpus adequacy / approval / evaluator\r\n// rule-checks from AuditIrRun via GET /api/inspector/runs/{id}) is a documented\r\n// follow-up — that endpoint is used by no frontend yet, so building on it would\r\n// violate LAW II until it's verified live against the backend.\r\n\r\nimport Link from \"next/link\";\r\n\r\nimport { BundlePendingCta } from \"@/components/inspector/bundle_pending_cta\";\r\nimport { Button } from \"@/components/ui/button\";\r\nimport {\r\n  loadBundle,\r\n  type LoadedBundle,\r\n  type VerifiedReportShape,\r\n} from \"@/lib/inspector_bundle_loader\";\r\nimport type { FileEntry } from \"@/lib/signed_bundle\";\r\n\r\ninterface AuditPageProps {\r\n  params: Promise<{ runId: string }>;\r\n}\r\n\r\nconst CONTENT_TYPE_LABEL: Record<string, string> = {\r\n  scope_decision: \"Scope decision\",\r\n  evidence_pool: \"Evidence pool\",\r\n  verified_report: \"Verified report\",\r\n  source_snapshot: \"Source snapshot\",\r\n  metadata: \"Metadata\",\r\n  reasoning_trace: \"Reasoning trace\",\r\n};\r\n\r\nfunction pct(value: number): string {\r\n  return `${Math.round(value * 100)}%`;\r\n}\r\n\r\nfunction shortHash(sha256: string): string {\r\n  return sha256.length > 16 ? `${sha256.slice(0, 16)}…` : sha256;\r\n}\r\n\r\n// I-p2-020 Codex iter-2 P1: `verified_sentences` carries BOTH passed AND\r\n// dropped sentences (audit completeness), so `.length` overstates coverage.\r\n// Count `verifier_pass === true` for the genuinely span-verified total.\r\nfunction passedSentences(section: {\r\n  verified_sentences: { verifier_pass: boolean }[];\r\n}): number {\r\n  return section.verified_sentences.filter((s) => s.verifier_pass).length;\r\n}\r\n\r\nfunction totalPassedSentences(report: VerifiedReportShape): number {\r\n  return report.sections.reduce(\r\n    (sum, section) => sum + passedSentences(section),\r\n    0,\r\n  );\r\n}\r\n\r\nfunction totalAttemptedSentences(report: VerifiedReportShape): number {\r\n  return report.sections.reduce(\r\n    (sum, section) => sum + section.verified_sentences.length,\r\n    0,\r\n  );\r\n}\r\n\r\ninterface GateRow {\r\n  gate: string;\r\n  pass: boolean;\r\n  detail: string;\r\n}\r\n\r\n// The pipeline gate ledger, composed ONLY from fields that exist in the proven\r\n// signed-bundle verified_report. No fabricated gate fields.\r\nfunction buildGateLedger(report: VerifiedReportShape): GateRow[] {\r\n  const rows: GateRow[] = [\r\n    {\r\n      gate: \"Pipeline verdict\",\r\n      pass: report.pipeline_verdict === \"success\",\r\n      detail: report.pipeline_verdict,\r\n    },\r\n    {\r\n      gate: \"Strict-verify (overall)\",\r\n      pass: report.overall_verify_pass_rate >= report.verifier_pass_threshold,\r\n      detail: `${pct(report.overall_verify_pass_rate)} of sentences span-verified · threshold ${pct(report.verifier_pass_threshold)}`,\r\n    },\r\n    {\r\n      gate: \"Two-family segregation\",\r\n      pass: report.family_segregation_passed,\r\n      // I-p2-020 Codex P2: don't hardcode \"≠\" when the gate failed (a\r\n      // same-lineage bundle would show a contradictory detail beside Fail).\r\n      detail: report.family_segregation_passed\r\n        ? `Distinct lineages — ${report.generator_model} (generator) ≠ ${report.evaluator_model} (evaluator)`\r\n        : `Same lineage — ${report.generator_model} (generator), ${report.evaluator_model} (evaluator)`,\r\n    },\r\n  ];\r\n  for (const section of report.sections) {\r\n    rows.push({\r\n      gate: `Section: ${section.section_id}`,\r\n      pass: section.section_verify_pass_rate >= report.verifier_pass_threshold,\r\n      detail: `${pct(section.section_verify_pass_rate)} span-verified · ${passedSentences(section)}/${section.verified_sentences.length} sentences passed`,\r\n    });\r\n  }\r\n  return rows;\r\n}\r\n\r\nfunction StatusPill({ pass }: { pass: boolean }) {\r\n  return (\r\n    <span\r\n      className={\r\n        pass\r\n          ? \"border-verified bg-verified/10 text-verified inline-flex items-center rounded-full border px-2 py-0.5 text-[10px] font-semibold tracking-wide uppercase\"\r\n          : \"border-refusal bg-refusal/10 text-refusal inline-flex items-center rounded-full border px-2 py-0.5 text-[10px] font-semibold tracking-wide uppercase\"\r\n      }\r\n    >\r\n      {pass ? \"Pass\" : \"Fail\"}\r\n    </span>\r\n  );\r\n}\r\n\r\nfunction MetaRow({ label, value }: { label: string; value: string }) {\r\n  return (\r\n    <div className=\"flex flex-col gap-0.5\">\r\n      <span className=\"text-muted-foreground text-[10px] font-medium tracking-widest uppercase\">\r\n        {label}\r\n      </span>\r\n      <span className=\"text-foreground text-sm break-words\">{value}</span>\r\n    </div>\r\n  );\r\n}\r\n\r\nfunction AuditExportBody({ bundle }: { bundle: LoadedBundle }) {\r\n  const { manifest, metadata, verifiedReport, signaturePresent, runId } =\r\n    bundle;\r\n  const gateLedger = buildGateLedger(verifiedReport);\r\n  const files: FileEntry[] = manifest.files;\r\n  const totalBytes = files.reduce((sum, f) => sum + f.size_bytes, 0);\r\n\r\n  return (\r\n    <div className=\"flex flex-col gap-8\">\r\n      {/* Integrity & provenance */}\r\n      <section className=\"border-border bg-card flex flex-col gap-4 rounded-lg border p-5\">\r\n        <div className=\"flex flex-wrap items-start justify-between gap-3\">\r\n          <h2 className=\"text-foreground text-sm font-semibold\">\r\n            Integrity &amp; provenance\r\n          </h2>\r\n          {/* HONEST signature status — never claims a seal that isn't on disk.\r\n              I-p2-020 Codex iter-3 P1: `signaturePresent` is only an .asc\r\n              PRESENCE check (loader), not a cryptographic verify — say\r\n              \"present\" + point to operator-side gpg --verify, never \"signed\". */}\r\n          {signaturePresent ? K : never\n}[keyof T]\n\ntype OnNavigateEventHandler = (event: { preventDefault: () => void }) => void\n\ntype InternalLinkProps = {\n  /**\n   * **Required**. The path or URL to navigate to. It can also be an object (similar to `URL`).\n   *\n   * @example\n   * ```tsx\n   * // Navigate to /dashboard:\n   * <Link href=\"/dashboard\">Dashboard</Link>\n   *\n   * // Navigate to /about?name=test:\n   * <Link href={{ pathname: '/about', query: { name: 'test' } }}>\n   *   About\n   * </Link>\n   * ```\n   *\n   * @remarks\n   * - For external URLs, use a fully qualified URL such as `https://...`.\n   * - In the App Router, dynamic routes must not include bracketed segments in `href`.\n   */\n  href: Url\n\n  /**\n   * @deprecated v10.0.0: `href` props pointing to a dynamic route are\n   * automatically resolved and no longer require the `as` prop.\n   */\n  as?: Url\n\n  /**\n   * Replace the current `history` state instead of adding a new URL into the stack.\n   *\n   * @defaultValue `false`\n   *\n   * @example\n   * ```tsx\n   * <Link href=\"/about\" replace>\n   *   About (replaces the history state)\n   * </Link>\n   * ```\n   */\n  replace?: boolean\n\n  /**\n   * Whether to override the default scroll behavior. If `true`, Next.js attempts to maintain\n   * the scroll position if the newly navigated page is still visible. If not, it scrolls to the top.\n   *\n   * If `false`, Next.js will not modify the scroll behavior at all.\n   *\n   * @defaultValue `true`\n   *\n   * @example\n   * ```tsx\n   * <Link href=\"/dashboard\" scroll={false}>\n   *   No auto scroll\n   * </Link>\n   * ```\n   */\n  scroll?: boolean\n\n  /**\n   * Update the path of the current page without rerunning data fetching methods\n   * like `getStaticProps`, `getServerSideProps`, or `getInitialProps`.\n   *\n   * @remarks\n   * `shallow` only applies to the Pages Router. For the App Router, see the\n   * [following documentation](https://nextjs.org/docs/app/building-your-application/routing/linking-and-navigating#using-the-native-history-api).\n   *\n   * @defaultValue `false`\n   *\n   * @example\n   * ```tsx\n   * <Link href=\"/blog\" shallow>\n   *   Shallow navigation\n   * </Link>\n   * ```\n   */\n  shallow?: boolean\n\n  /**\n   * Forces `Link` to pass its `href` to the child component. Useful if the child is a custom\n   * component that wraps an `<a>` tag, or if you're using certain styling libraries.\n   *\n   * @defaultValue `false`\n   *\n   * @example\n   * ```tsx\n   * <Link href=\"/dashboard\" passHref legacyBehavior>\n   *   <MyStyledAnchor>Dashboard</MyStyledAnchor>\n   * </Link>\n   * ```\n   */\n  passHref?: boolean\n\n  /**\n   * Prefetch the page in the background.\n   * Any `<Link />` that is in the viewport (initially or through scroll) will be prefetched.\n   * Prefetch can be disabled by passing `prefetch={false}`.\n   *\n   * @remarks\n   * Prefetching is only enabled in production.\n   *\n   * - In the **App Router**:\n   *   - `\"auto\"`, `null`, `undefined` (default): Prefetch behavior depends on static vs dynamic routes:\n   *     - Static routes: fully prefetched\n   *     - Dynamic routes: partial prefetch to the nearest segment with a `loading.js`\n   *   - `true`: Always prefetch the full route and data.\n   *   - `false`: Disable prefetching on both viewport and hover.\n   * - In the **Pages Router**:\n   *   - `true` (default): Prefetches the route and data in the background on viewport or hover.\n   *   - `false`: Prefetch only on hover, not on viewport.\n   *\n   * @defaultValue `true` (Pages Router) or `null` (App Router)\n   *\n   * @example\n   * ```tsx\n   * <Link href=\"/dashboard\" prefetch={false}>\n   *   Dashboard\n   * </Link>\n   * ```\n   */\n  prefetch?: boolean | 'auto' | null\n\n  /**\n   * (unstable) Switch to a full prefetch on hover.       }\n      }\n\n      if (!legacyBehavior && typeof onClick === 'function') {\n        onClick(e)\n      }\n\n      if (\n        legacyBehavior &&\n        child.props &&\n        typeof containerStyles.scrollPaddingLeft;\n      }\n    }\n    if (direction === 'rtl') {\n      if (elementOffsetLeft - elementStyles.scrollMarginRight < scrollContainer.scrollLeft + Hard-coded\r\n * thresholds; production reads from a regression-config file (post-Carney).\r\n *\r\n * I-f13-004: source-retraction context attribution for sentence-count drops.\r\n */\r\n\r\nimport type { PinSnapshot } from \"@/lib/pin_replay_demo\";\r\n\r\nexport const REGRESSION_THRESHOLDS = {\r\n  pass_rate_pct_drop: 5,\r\n  verified_sentence_count_drop: 3,\r\n} as const;\r\n\r\nexport interface RegressionAlert {\r\n  metric: \"pass_rate\" | \"verified_sentence_count\";\r\n  a_value: number;\r\n  b_value: number;\r\n  drop: number;\r\n  threshold: number;\r\n  unit: \"pct\" | \"count\";\r\n  attributed_to_retraction?: string[];\r\n}\r\n\r\nexport interface RetractionContext {\r\n  newly_retracted: string[];\r\n}\r\n\r\nexport function getRetractionContext(\r\n  a: PinSnapshot,\r\n  b: PinSnapshot,\r\n): RetractionContext {\r\n  const a_ids = a.retracted_source_ids ?? [];\r\n  const newly_retracted = (b.retracted_source_ids ?? []).filter(\r\n    (id) => !a_ids.includes(id),\r\n  );\r\n  return { newly_retracted };\r\n}\r\n\r\nexport function detectRegressions(\r\n  a: PinSnapshot,\r\n  b: PinSnapshot,\r\n): RegressionAlert[] {\r\n  const alerts: RegressionAlert[] = [];\r\n  const retraction = getRetractionContext(a, b);\r\n\r\n  const a_pass_pct = Math.round(a.pass_rate * 100);\r\n  const b_pass_pct = Math.round(b.pass_rate * 100);\r\n  const pass_drop = a_pass_pct - b_pass_pct;\r\n  if (pass_drop > REGRESSION_THRESHOLDS.pass_rate_pct_drop) {\r\n    alerts.push({\r\n      metric: \"pass_rate\",\r\n      a_value: a_pass_pct,\r\n      b_value: b_pass_pct,\r\n      drop: pass_drop,\r\n      threshold: REGRESSION_THRESHOLDS.pass_rate_pct_drop,\r\n      unit: \"pct\",\r\n    });\r\n  }\r\n\r\n  const sentence_drop = a.verified_sentence_count - b.verified_sentence_count;\r\n  if (sentence_drop > REGRESSION_THRESHOLDS.verified_sentence_count_drop) {\r\n    const alert: RegressionAlert = {\r\n      metric: \"verified_sentence_count\",\r\n      a_value: a.verified_sentence_count,\r\n      b_value: b.verified_sentence_count,\r\n      drop: sentence_drop,\r\n      threshold: REGRESSION_THRESHOLDS.verified_sentence_count_drop,\r\n      unit: \"count\",\r\n    };\r\n    if (retraction.newly_retracted.length > 0) {\r\n      alert.attributed_to_retraction = retraction.newly_retracted;\r\n    }\r\n    alerts.push(alert);\r\n  }\r\n\r\n  return alerts;\r\n}\r\n","\"use client\";\r\n\r\nimport {\r\n  Sheet,\r\n  SheetContent,\r\n  SheetDescription,\r\n  SheetHeader,\r\n  SheetTitle,\r\n} from \"@/components/ui/sheet\";\r\nimport type { PinSnapshot } from \"@/lib/pin_replay_demo\";\r\n\r\ntype FieldKind = \"numeric\" | \"string\";\r\n\r\nconst FIELDS: { key: keyof PinSnapshot; label: string; kind: FieldKind }[] = [\r\n  { key: \"query\", label: \"Query\", kind: \"string\" },\r\n  { key: \"verdict\", label: \"Verdict\", kind: \"string\" },\r\n  { key: \"section_count_kept\", label: \"Sections kept\", kind: \"numeric\" },\r\n  { key: \"section_count_dropped\", label: \"Sections dropped\", kind: \"numeric\" },\r\n  {\r\n    key: \"verified_sentence_count\",\r\n    label: \"Verified sentences\",\r\n    kind: \"numeric\",\r\n  },\r\n  { key: \"pass_rate\", label: \"Pass rate (%)\", kind: \"numeric\" },\r\n];\r\n\r\nfunction formatValue(value: unknown, kind: FieldKind, key: string): string {\r\n  if (kind === \"numeric\" && typeof value === \"number\") {\r\n    if (key === \"pass_rate\") return `${Math.round(value * 100)}%`;\r\n    return String(value);\r\n  }\r\n  return String(value ?? \"\");\r\n}\r\n\r\nfunction computeDelta(\r\n  a: unknown,\r\n  b: unknown,\r\n  kind: FieldKind,\r\n  key: string,\r\n): string {\r\n  if (kind === \"numeric\" && typeof a === \"number\" && typeof b === \"number\") {\r\n    const delta =\r\n      key === \"pass_rate\" ? Math.round(b * 100) - Math.round(a * 100) : b - a;\r\n    const prefix = delta > 0 ? \"+\" : \"\";\r\n    return key === \"pass_rate\" ? `${prefix}${delta}%` : `${prefix}${delta}`;\r\n  }\r\n  // String/categorical: report changed/unchanged.\r\n  return a === b ? \"(unchanged)\" : \"(changed)\";\r\n}\r\n\r\nexport function DiffSidePanel({\r\n  open,\r\n  onOpenChange,\r\n  snapshot_a,\r\n  snapshot_b,\r\n}: {\r\n  open: boolean;\r\n  onOpenChange: (next: boolean) => void;\r\n  snapshot_a: PinSnapshot | null;\r\n  snapshot_b: PinSnapshot | null;\r\n}) {\r\n  return (\r\n    <Sheet open={open} onOpenChange={onOpenChange}>\r\n      <SheetContent\r\n        data-testid=\"pin-diff-pane\"\r\n        side=\"right\"\r\n        className=\"data-[side=right]:w-full data-[side=right]:sm:w-2/5 data-[side=right]:sm:max-w-none\"\r\n      >\r\n        <SheetHeader>\r\n          <SheetTitle>Snapshot diff (B − A)</SheetTitle>\r\n          <SheetDescription>\r\n            Per-field comparison of snapshot A and snapshot B.\r\n          </SheetDescription>\r\n        </SheetHeader>\r\n        {snapshot_a && snapshot_b ? (\r\n          <div className=\"px-4 pb-4 text-sm\">\r\n            <table className=\"w-full text-xs\">\r\n              <thead>\r\n                <tr className=\"border-border border-b\">\r\n                  <th className=\"py-1 text-left\">Field</th>\r\n                  <th className=\"py-1 text-left\">A</th>\r\n                  <th className=\"py-1 text-left\">B</th>\r\n                  <th className=\"py-1 text-left\">Δ</th>\r\n                </tr>\r\n              </thead>\r\n              <tbody>\r\n                {FIELDS.map(({ key, label, kind }) => (\r\n                  <tr\r\n                    key={key}\r\n                    data-testid={`pin-diff-row-${String(key)}`}\r\n                    className=\"border-border border-b\"\r\n                  >\r\n                    <td className=\"py-1 font-medium\">{label}</td>\r\n                    <td className=\"truncate py-1\">\r\n                      {formatValue(snapshot_a[key], kind, String(key))}\r\n                    </td>\r\n                    <td className=\"truncate py-1\">\r\n                      {formatValue(snapshot_b[key], kind, String(key))}\r\n                    </td>\r\n                    <td\r\n                      data-testid={`pin-diff-delta-${String(key)}`}\r\n                      className=\"py-1\"\r\n                    >\r\n                      {computeDelta(\r\n                        snapshot_a[key],\r\n                        snapshot_b[key],\r\n                        kind,\r\n                        String(key),\r\n                      )}\r\n                    </td>\r\n                  </tr>\r\n                ))}\r\n              </tbody>\r\n            </table>\r\n          </div>\r\n        ) : (\r\n          <p\r\n       )}\r\n      </SheetContent>\r\n    </Sheet>\r\n  );\r\n}\r\n","/**\r\n * I-f10-004: TypeScript helper that mirrors `polaris_v6.charts.spec_builder.build_timeline`\r\n * for client-side visualization demos. Canonical spec generator remains the\r\n * Python builder; the backend route `/runs/{run_id}/charts/timeline` returns\r\n * specs of the same shape this helper produces.\r\n */\r\n\r\nimport type { VegaLiteSpec } from \"@/lib/api\";\r\n\r\nexport const VEGA_LITE_SCHEMA_URL =\r\n  \"https://vega.github.io/schema/vega-lite/v5.json\";\r\n\r\nexport type TimelinePeriodKind = \"date\" | \"quarter\" | \"year\";\r\n\r\nexport interface TimelinePoint {\r\n  period: string;\r\n  value: number;\r\n  series: string;\r\n  evidence_id: string;\r\n}\r\n\r\nexport function buildTimelineSpec(\r\n  title: string,\r\n  points: TimelinePoint[],\r\n  period_kind: TimelinePeriodKind = \"quarter\",\r\n): VegaLiteSpec {\r\n  if (points.length === 0) {\r\n    throw new Error(\"timeline requires at least one point\");\r\n  }\r\n  const temporal_type = period_kind === \"date\" ? \"temporal\" : \"ordinal\";\r\n  const data_values = points.map((p) => ({\r\n    period: p.period,\r\n    value: p.value,\r\n    series: p.series,\r\n    evidence_id: p.evidence_id,\r\n  }));\r\n  return {\r\n    $schema: VEGA_LITE_SCHEMA_URL,\r\n    title,\r\n    data: { values: data_values },\r\n    mark: { type: \"line\", point: true },\r\n    encoding: {\r\n      x: {\r\n        field: \"period\",\r\n        type: temporal_type,\r\n        axis: { title: \"Period\" },\r\n      },\r\n      y: { field: \"value\", type: \"quantitative\" },\r\n      color: { field: \"series\", type: \"nominal\" },\r\n      tooltip: [\r\n        { field: \"period\" },\r\n        { field: \"value\" },\r\n        { field: \"series\" },\r\n        { field: \"evidence_id\" },\r\n      ],\r\n    },\r\n    polaris_provenance: {\r\n      chart_type: \"timeline\",\r\n      period_kind,\r\n      evidence_ids: points.map((p) => p.evidence_id),\r\n    },\r\n  };\r\n}\r\n","\"use client\";\r\n\r\nimport { VegaChart } from \"@/components/ui/vega-chart\";\r\nimport { buildTimelineSpec, type TimelinePoint } from \"@/lib/timeline_spec\";\r\n\r\nimport type { PinSnapshot } from \"@/lib/pin_replay_demo\";\r\n\r\nexport function PinTimeseries({ snapshots }: { snapshots: PinSnapshot[] }) {\r\n  const sorted = [...snapshots].sort((a, b) =>\r\n    a.pin_date.localeCompare(b.pin_date),\r\n  );\r\n\r\n  const pass_rate_points: TimelinePoint[] = sorted.map((s) => ({\r\n    period: s.pin_date,\r\n    value: Math.round(s.pass_rate * 100),\r\n    series: \"pass_rate_pct\",\r\n    evidence_id: `demo-pin-${s.pin_date}-pass`,\r\n  }));\r\n\r\n  const sentences_points: TimelinePoint[] = sorted.map((s) => ({\r\n    period: s.pin_date,\r\n    value: s.verified_sentence_count,\r\n    series: \"verified_sentences\",\r\n    evidence_id: `demo-pin-${s.pin_date}-sentences`,\r\n  }));\r\n\r\n  const pass_rate_spec = buildTimelineSpec(\r\n    \"Pass rate over time (%)\",\r\n    pass_rate_points,\r\n    \"date\",\r\n  );\r\n  const sentences_spec = buildTimelineSpec(\r\n    \"Verified sentences over time\",\r\n    sentences_points,\r\n    \"date\",\r\n  );\r\n\r\n  return (\r\n    <div className=\"grid grid-cols-1 gap-4 md:grid-cols-2\">\r\n      <section\r\n        data-testid=\"pin-timeseries-pass-rate\"\r\n        className=\"flex flex-col gap-2\"\r\n      >\r\n        <h3 className=\"text-sm font-medium\">Pass rate (%)</h3>\r\n        <VegaChart .next\server\chunks\ssr\_01ugdov._.js.map:1:{"version":3,"sources":["../../../../lib/api.ts","../../../../components/ui/card.tsx","../../../../app/retrieval/components/retrieval_runner.tsx","../../../../app/retrieval/components/corpus_brief.tsx"],"sourcesContent":["/**\r\n * POLARIS v6 API client.\r\n *\r\n * Mirrors the FastAPI contract in src/polaris_v6/api/{runs,health,stream}.py.\r\n *\r\n * I-carney-005 (2026-05-13): all requests are browser-relative paths so the\r\n * browser only ever talks to the webui origin. Next.js server-side rewrites\r\n * (web/next.config.ts) forward `/api/v6/*` → `${INTERNAL_API_URL}/*`, which\r\n * in production compose is `http://api:8000` (a Docker service name the\r\n * browser cannot resolve directly). In local dev, INTERNAL_API_URL defaults\r\n * to http://localhost:8000 so `npm run dev` against a host-port-mapped\r\n * backend works without any client-side configuration.\r\n *\r\n * Codex diff iter-1 P1-B: NEXT_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL is no longer consulted at\r\n * runtime — baking an absolute http://api:8000 into the client bundle would\r\n * make production browsers attempt direct Docker-network reach and fail.\r\n */\r\n\r\nimport { authHeader, redirectToSignIn } from \"@/lib/auth\";\r\n\r\nconst BACKEND_URL = \"/api/v6\";\r\n\r\n/**\r\n * I-rdy-004 (#500) — fetch wrapper for every v6 API call. Injects the\r\n * `Authorization: Bearer <jwt>` header, preserves any caller-supplied\r\n * headers, and does NOT force a content-type (so `FormData` uploads keep\r\n * their auto-generated multipart boundary). On a 401 it clears the token\r\n * and routes to /sign-in.\r\n */\r\nasync function authFetch(\r\n  url: string,\r\n  init: RequestInit = {},\r\n): Promise<Response> {\r\n  const headers = new Headers(init.headers ?? {});\r\n  for (const [key, value] of Object.entries(authHeader())) {\r\n    headers.set(key, value);\r\n  }\r\n  const response = await globalThis.fetch(url, { ...init, headers });\r\n  if (response.status === 401) {\r\n    redirectToSignIn();\r\n  }\r\n  return response;\r\n}\r\n\r\n/**\r\n * I-rdy-004 — SSE URL for a run's event stream. Native `EventSource` cannot\r\n * set request headers, so the JWT rides as the `access_token` query param;\r\n * the backend `require_auth` accepts it for `/stream/*` paths only.\r\n */\r\nexport function streamUrl(runId: string): string {\r\n  const token = authHeader().Authorization?.replace(/^Bearer /, \"\");\r\n  const base = `${BACKEND_URL}/stream/${encodeURIComponent(runId)}`;\r\n  return token ? `${base}?access_token=${encodeURIComponent(token)}` : base;\r\n}\r\n\r\nexport type TemplateId =\r\n  | \"clinical\"\r\n  | \"policy\"\r\n  | \"tech\"\r\n  | \"due_diligence\"\r\n  | \"ai_sovereignty\"\r\n  | \"canada_us\"\r\n  | \"workforce\"\r\n  | \"custom\";\r\n\r\nexport type RunStatus =\r\n  | \"queued\"\r\n  | \"in_progress\"\r\n  | \"completed\"\r\n  | \"cancelled\"\r\n  | \"failed\"\r\n  | \"abort_scope_rejected\"\r\n  | \"abort_corpus_inadequate\"\r\n  | \"abort_corpus_approval_denied\"\r\n  | \"abort_no_verified_sections\";\r\n\r\nexport interface RunRequest {\r\n  template: TemplateId;\r\n  question: string;\r\n  document_ids?: string[];\r\n}\r\n\r\nexport interface RunStatusResponse {\r\n  run_id: string;\r\n  status: RunStatus;\r\n  // I-ui-bug-001 (#725): the pipeline-A verdict (success / abort_* / partial_*\r\n  // / error_*), distinct from the lifecycle `status`. mark_aborted() stores\r\n  // lifecycle_status='completed' for aborts, so lifecycle alone CANNOT tell a\r\n  // successful run from an aborted one — pipeline_status is required to render\r\n  // a completed run honestly (an aborted run must NOT show as success).\r\n  pipeline_status?: string | null;\r\n  // I-rdy-011 (#507): true once a cancel has been requested; lets the run\r\n  // page show a cancel as in-flight before an in-progress run transitions.\r\n  cancel_requested?: boolean;\r\n  template: string;\r\n  question: string;\r\n  queued_at: string;\r\n  started_at?: string | null;\r\n  finished_at?: string | null;\r\n}\r\n\r\nexport interface ApiError extends Error {\r\n  status: number;\r\n  body: unknown;\r\n}\r\n\r\nasync function asJsonOrThrow<T>(response: Response): Promise<T> {\r\n  const body = await response.json().catch(() => null);\r\n  if (!response.ok) {\r\n    const error = new Error(\r\n      `POLARIS backend returned ${response.status}`,\r\n    ) as ApiError;\r\n    error.status = response.status;\r\n    error.body = body;\r\n    throw error;\r\n  }\r\n  return body as T;\r\n}\r\n\r\n/**\r\n * Thrown by createRun when POST /runs is rejected by the 1-concurrent-session\r\n * constraint (I-rdy-013): a research run is already queued or in progress.\r\n */\r\nexport class ConcurrentRunError extends Error {\r\n  readonly activeRunId: string;\r\n  readonly activeStatus: string;\r\n  constructor(message: string, activeRunId: string, activeStatus: string) {\r\n    super(message);\r\n    this.name = \"ConcurrentRunError\";\r\n    this.activeRunId = activeRunId;\r\n    this.activeStatus = activeStatus;\r\n  }\r\n}\r\n\r\nexport async function createRun(\r\n  payload: RunRequest,\r\n): Promise<RunStatusResponse> {\r\n  const response = await authFetch(`${BACKEND_URL}/runs`, {\r\n    method: \"POST\",\r\n    headers: { \"content-type\": \"application/json\" },\r\n    body: JSON.stringify(payload),\r\n  });\r\n  if (response.status === 409) {\r\n    // Read the body exactly once, then unwrap FastAPI's { detail: {...} }\r\n    // envelope. The 1-concurrent-session reject carries a structured detail;\r\n    // any other 409 falls through to a generic ApiError.\r\n    const body = (await response.json().catch(() => null)) as {\r\n      detail?: unknown;\r\n    } | null;\r\n    const detail = (body?.detail ?? body) as {\r\n      code?: string;\r\n      message?: string;\r\n      active_run_id?: string;\r\n      active_status?: string;\r\n    } | null;\r\n    if (detail && detail.code === \"concurrent_run_active\") {\r\n      throw new ConcurrentRunError(\r\n        detail.message ?? \"A research run is already in progress.\",\r\n        detail.active_run_id ?? \"\",\r\n        detail.active_status ?? \"in_progress\",\r\n      );\r\n    }\r\n    const error = new Error(\"POLARIS backend returned 409\") as ApiError;\r\n    error.status = 409;\r\n    error.body = body;\r\n    throw error;\r\n  }\r\n  return asJsonOrThrow<RunStatusResponse>(response);\r\n}\r\n\r\nexport async function getRun(runId: string): Promise<RunStatusResponse> {\r\n  const response = await authFetch(`${BACKEND_URL}/runs/${runId}`);\r\n  return asJsonOrThrow<RunStatusResponse>(response);\r\n}\r\n\r\n// I-rdy-011 (#507): request cancellation of a run. A queued run cancels\r\n// instantly; an in-progress run aborts cooperatively at the next pipeline\r\n// stage boundary. Already-terminal runs are an idempotent no-op.\r\nexport async function cancelRun(runId: string): Promise<RunStatusResponse> {\r\n  const response = await authFetch(\r\n    `${BACKEND_URL}/runs/${encodeURIComponent(runId)}/cancel`,\r\n    { method: \"POST\" },\r\n  );\r\n  return asJsonOrThrow<RunStatusResponse>(response);\r\n}\r\n\r\n// I-ui-003 (#542): report-scoped follow-up. Returns the EventSource so the caller can\r\n * close it. Per Phase 0 stub, the backend yields 5 deterministic events.\r\n */\r\nexport function subscribeToRun(\r\n  runId: string,\r\n  onEvent: (event: StreamEvent) => void,\r\n  onError?: (error: Event) => void,\r\n): EventSource {\r\n  const source = new EventSource(streamUrl(runId));\r\n\r\n  const handler = (event: MessageEvent<string>) => {\r\n    try {\r\n      const parsed = JSON.parse(event.data);\r\n      onEvent({ event: event.type, data: parsed });\r\n    } catch {\r\n      onEvent({ event: event.type, data: { raw: event.data } });\r\n    }\r\n  };\r\n\r\n  for (const eventName of [\r\n    \"scope_decision\",\r\n    \"retrieval_progress\",\r\n    // I-ui-002 (#707): evidence_id drives the retrieval source feed +\r\n    // sources-read counter; it was previously emitted by the backend but\r\n    // never listened for here, so those SSE events were silently dropped.\r\n    \"evidence_id\",\r\n    \"verifier_verdict\",\r\n    \"section_complete\",\r\n    \"run_complete\",\r\n  ]) {\r\n    source.addEventListener(eventName, handler);\r\n  }\r\n\r\n  if (onError) {\r\n    source.onerror = onError;\r\n  }\r\n  return source;\r\n}\r\n\r\n// RetrievalSourceTier,\r\n): number {\r\n  return pool.sources.filter((s) => s.tier === tier).length;\r\n}\r\n\r\n/** Convenience: dedupe + sort sources by tier (T1 first), then by domain. */\r\nexport function sortSourcesByTier(\r\n  sources: RetrievalSource[],\r\n): RetrievalSource[] {\r\n  const tier_rank: Record<RetrievalSourceTier, number> = {\r\n    T1: 0,\r\n    T2: 1,\r\n    T3: 2,\r\n  };\r\n  return [...sources].sort((a, b) => {\r\n    const t = tier_rank[a.tier] - tier_rank[b.tier];\r\n    if (t !== 0) return t;\r\n    return a.domain.localeCompare(b.domain);\r\n  });\r\n}\r\n\r\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\r\n// Slice 003 — Generator with strict-verify\r\n// Mirrors scope_class: scope_class ?? null }),\r\n  });\r\n\r\n  if (response.status === 400) {\r\n    const detail = await response.json().catch(() => null);\r\n    const body = (detail?.detail ?? detail) as \"@/components/ui/card\";\r\nimport {\r\n  IntakeBadRequestError,\r\n  RetrievalBadRequestError,\r\n  runIntake,\r\n  runRetrieval,\r\n  type EvidencePool,\r\n  type IntakeScopeDecision,\r\n} from \"@/lib/api\";\r\n\r\nimport { CorpusBrief } from \"./corpus_brief\";\r\n\r\ntype RunnerState =\r\n  | { kind: \"idle\" }\r\n  | { kind: \"scope_loading\" }\r\n  | { kind: \"scope_failed\"; message: string }\r\n  | {\r\n      kind: \"scope_unsuitable\";\r\n      decision: IntakeScopeDecision;\r\n      reason: string;\r\n    }\r\n  | {\r\n      kind: \"retrieval_loading\";\r\n      decision: IntakeScopeDecision;\r\n    }\r\n  | {\r\n      kind: \"retrieval_failed\";\r\n      decision: IntakeScopeDecision;\r\n      code: string;\r\n      message: string;\r\n    }\r\n  | {\r\n      kind: \"ok\";\r\n      decision: IntakeScopeDecision;\r\n      pool: EvidencePool;\r\n    };\r\n\r\nexport function RetrievalRunner() {\r\n  const [question, setQuestion] = useState(\"\");\r\n  const [state, setState] = useState<RunnerState>({ kind: \"idle\" });\r\n\r\n  async function submit(e: React.FormEvent<HTMLFormElement>) {\r\n    e.preventDefault();\r\n    const trimmed = question.trim();\r\n    if (trimmed.length < 3) {\r\n      setState({ kind: \"scope_failed\", message: \"Question is too short.\" });\r\n      return;\r\n    }\r\n\r\n    setState({ kind: \"scope_loading\" });\r\n    let decision: IntakeScopeDecision;\r\n    try {\r\n      const intake = await runIntake(trimmed);\r\n      decision = intake.decision;\r\n    } catch (err) {\r\n      const msg =\r\n        err instanceof IntakeBadRequestError\r\n          ? err.message\r\n          : err instanceof Error\r\n            ? err.message\r\n            : \"Intake failed.\";\r\n      setState({ kind: \"scope_failed\", message: msg });\r\n      return;\r\n    }\r\n\r\n    if (decision.status !== \"in_scope\") {\r\n      setState({\r\n        kind: \"scope_unsuitable\",\r\n        decision,\r\n        reason: scope_reason_for(decision),\r\n      });\r\n      return;\r\n    }\r\n\r\n    setState({ kind: \"retrieval_loading\", decision });\r\n    try {\r\n      const result = await runRetrieval(decision);\r\n      setState({ kind: \"ok\", decision, pool: result.pool });\r\n    } catch (err) {\r\n      if (err instanceof RetrievalBadRequestError) {\r\n        setState({\r\n          kind: \"retrieval_failed\",\r\n          decision,\r\n          code: err.code,\r\n          message: err.message,\r\n        });\r\n        return;\r\n      }\r\n      const msg = err instanceof Error ? err.message : \"Retrieval failed.\";\r\n      setState({\r\n        kind: \"retrieval_failed\",\r\n        decision,\r\n        code: \"unknown\",\r\n        message: msg,\r\n      });\r\n    }\r\n  }\r\n\r\n  return (\r\n    <div className=\"flex flex-col gap-6\">\r\n      <Card>\r\n        <CardHeader>\r\n          <CardTitle className=\"text-xl\">Retrieve clinical evidence</CardTitle>\r\n        </CardHeader>\r\n        <CardContent>\r\n          <form\r\n            onSubmit={submit}\r\n            className=\"flex flex-col gap-3\"\r\n            data-testid=\"retrieval-form\"\r\n          >\r\n            <label\r\n              htmlFor=\"retrieval-question\"\r\n              className=\"text-foreground text-sm font-medium\"\r\n            >\r\n              Clinical research question\r\n            </label>\r\n            <input\r\n              id=\"retrieval-question\"\r\n              data-testid=\"retrieval-question-input\"\r\n              type=\"text\"\r\n              value={question}\r\n              onChange={(e) => setQuestion(e.target.value)}\r\n              placeholder=\"e.g. Is aspirin effective for headache in adults?\"\r\n              maxLength={2000}\r\n              autoComplete=\"off\"\r\n              disabled={\r\n                state.kind === \"scope_loading\" ||\r\n                state.kind === \"retrieval_loading\"\r\n              }\r\n              className=\"border-input focus-visible:border-ring focus-visible:ring-ring/70 h-9 w-full rounded-lg border bg-transparent px-3 py-1 text-sm transition-colors outline-none focus-visible:ring-3 disabled:opacity-50\"\r\n            />\r\n            <p className=\"text-muted-foreground text-xs\">\r\n              Slice 002 only retrieves for in-scope clinical questions. The\r\n              intake gate runs first; ambiguous, out-of-scope, or refused\r\n              questions short-circuit before any retrieval is attempted.\r\n            </p>\r\n            <Button\r\n              type=\"submit\"\r\n              variant=\"default\"\r\n              data-testid=\"retrieval-submit\"\r\n              disabled={\r\n                state.kind === \"scope_loading\" ||\r\n                state.kind === \"retrieval_loading\"\r\n              }\r\n            >\r\n              {state.kind === \"scope_loading\"\r\n                ? \"Checking scope…\"\r\n                : state.kind === \"retrieval_loading\"\r\n                  ? \"Retrieving sources…\"\r\n                  : \"Run retrieval\"}\r\n            </Button>\r\n          </form>\r\n        </CardContent>\r\n      </Card>\r\n\r\n      {state.kind === \"scope_failed\" ? (\r\n        <Card\r\n          data-testid=\"scope-unsuitable\"\r\n          className=\"border-amber-500/40 bg-amber-500/5\"\r\n        >\r\n          <CardContent className=\"text-amber-700 dark:text-amber-300\">\r\n            {state.reason}\r\n          </CardContent>\r\n        </Card>\r\n      ) : null}\r\n\r\n      {state.kind === \"retrieval_failed\" ?
