In local dev, INTERNAL_API_URL defaults\r\n * to http://localhost:8000 so `npm run dev` against a host-port-mapped\r\n * backend works without any client-side configuration.\r\n *\r\n * Codex diff iter-1 P1-B: NEXT_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL is no longer consulted at\r\n * runtime — baking an absolute http://api:8000 into the client bundle would\r\n * make production browsers attempt direct Docker-network reach and fail.\r\n */\r\n\r\nimport { authHeader, redirectToSignIn } from \"@/lib/auth\";\r\n\r\nconst BACKEND_URL = \"/api/v6\";\r\n\r\n/**\r\n * I-rdy-004 (#500) — fetch wrapper for every v6 API call. Injects the\r\n * `Authorization: Bearer <jwt>` header, preserves any caller-supplied\r\n * headers, and does NOT force a content-type (so `FormData` uploads keep\r\n * their auto-generated multipart boundary). On a 401 it clears the token\r\n * and routes to /sign-in.\r\n */\r\nasync function authFetch(\r\n  url: string,\r\n  init: RequestInit = {},\r\n): Promise<Response> {\r\n  const headers = new Headers(init.headers ?? {});\r\n  for (const [key, value] of Object.entries(authHeader())) {\r\n    headers.set(key, value);\r\n  }\r\n  const response = await globalThis.fetch(url, { ...init, headers });\r\n  if (response.status === 401) {\r\n    redirectToSignIn();\r\n  }\r\n  return response;\r\n}\r\n\r\n/**\r\n * I-rdy-004 — SSE URL for a run's event stream. Native `EventSource` cannot\r\n * set request headers, so the JWT rides as the `access_token` query param;\r\n * the backend `require_auth` accepts it for `/stream/*` paths only.\r\n */\r\nexport function streamUrl(runId: string): string {\r\n  const token = authHeader().Authorization?.replace(/^Bearer /, \"\");\r\n  const base = `${BACKEND_URL}/stream/${encodeURIComponent(runId)}`;\r\n  return token ? `${base}?access_token=${encodeURIComponent(token)}` : base;\r\n}\r\n\r\nexport type TemplateId =\r\n  | \"clinical\"\r\n  | \"policy\"\r\n  | \"tech\"\r\n  | \"due_diligence\"\r\n  | \"ai_sovereignty\"\r\n  | \"canada_us\"\r\n  | \"workforce\"\r\n  | \"custom\";\r\n\r\nexport type RunStatus =\r\n  | \"queued\"\r\n  | \"in_progress\"\r\n  | \"completed\"\r\n  | \"cancelled\"\r\n  | \"failed\"\r\n  | \"abort_scope_rejected\"\r\n  | \"abort_corpus_inadequate\"\r\n  | \"abort_corpus_approval_denied\"\r\n  | \"abort_no_verified_sections\";\r\n\r\nexport interface RunRequest {\r\n  template: TemplateId;\r\n  question: string;\r\n  document_ids?: string[];\r\n}\r\n\r\nexport interface RunStatusResponse {\r\n  run_id: string;\r\n  status: RunStatus;\r\n  // I-ui-bug-001 (#725): the pipeline-A verdict (success / abort_* / partial_*\r\n  // / error_*), distinct from the lifecycle `status`. mark_aborted() stores\r\n  // lifecycle_status='completed' for aborts, so lifecycle alone CANNOT tell a\r\n  // successful run from an aborted one — pipeline_status is required to render\r\n  // a completed run honestly (an aborted run must NOT show as success).\r\n  pipeline_status?: string | null;\r\n  // I-rdy-011 (#507): true once a cancel has been requested; lets the run\r\n  // page show a cancel as in-flight before an in-progress run transitions.\r\n  cancel_requested?: boolean;\r\n  template: string;\r\n  question: string;\r\n  queued_at: string;\r\n  started_at?: string | null;\r\n  finished_at?: string | null;\r\n}\r\n\r\nexport interface ApiError extends Error {\r\n  status: number;\r\n  body: unknown;\r\n}\r\n\r\nasync function asJsonOrThrow<T>(response: Response): Promise<T> {\r\n  const body = await response.json().catch(() => null);\r\n  if (!response.ok) {\r\n    const error = new Error(\r\n      `POLARIS backend returned ${response.status}`,\r\n    ) as ApiError;\r\n    error.status = response.status;\r\n    error.body = body;\r\n    throw error;\r\n  }\r\n  return body as T;\r\n}\r\n\r\n/**\r\n * Thrown by createRun when POST /runs is rejected by the 1-concurrent-session\r\n * constraint (I-rdy-013): a research run is already queued or in progress.\r\n */\r\nexport class ConcurrentRunError extends Error {\r\n  readonly activeRunId: string;\r\n  readonly activeStatus: string;\r\n  constructor(message: string, activeRunId: string, activeStatus: string) {\r\n    super(message);\r\n    this.name = \"ConcurrentRunError\";\r\n    this.activeRunId = activeRunId;\r\n    this.activeStatus = activeStatus;\r\n  }\r\n}\r\n\r\nexport async function createRun(\r\n  payload: RunRequest,\r\n): Promise<RunStatusResponse> {\r\n  const response = await authFetch(`${BACKEND_URL}/runs`, {\r\n    method: \"POST\",\r\n    headers: { \"content-type\": \"application/json\" },\r\n    body: JSON.stringify(payload),\r\n  });\r\n  if (response.status === 409) {\r\n    // Read the body exactly once, then unwrap FastAPI's { detail: {...} }\r\n    // envelope. The 1-concurrent-session reject carries a structured detail;\r\n    // any other 409 falls through to a generic ApiError.\r\n    const body = (await response.json().catch(() => null)) as {\r\n      detail?: unknown;\r\n    } | null;\r\n    const detail = (body?.detail ?? body) as {\r\n      code?: string;\r\n      message?: string;\r\n      active_run_id?: string;\r\n      active_status?: string;\r\n    } | null;\r\n    if (detail && detail.code === \"concurrent_run_active\") {\r\n      throw new ConcurrentRunError(\r\n        detail.message ?? \"A research run is already in progress.\",\r\n        detail.active_run_id ?? \"\",\r\n        detail.active_status ?? \"in_progress\",\r\n      );\r\n    }\r\n    const error = new Error(\"POLARIS backend returned 409\") as ApiError;\r\n    error.status = 409;\r\n    error.body = body;\r\n    throw error;\r\n  }\r\n  return asJsonOrThrow<RunStatusResponse>(response);\r\n}\r\n\r\nexport async function getRun(runId: string): Promise<RunStatusResponse> {\r\n  const response = await authFetch(`${BACKEND_URL}/runs/${runId}`);\r\n  return asJsonOrThrow<RunStatusResponse>(response);\r\n}\r\n\r\n// I-rdy-011 (#507): request cancellation of a run. A queued run cancels\r\n// instantly; an in-progress run aborts cooperatively at the next pipeline\r\n// stage boundary. Already-terminal runs are an idempotent no-op.\r\nexport async function cancelRun(runId: string): Promise<RunStatusResponse> {\r\n  const response = await authFetch(\r\n    `${BACKEND_URL}/runs/${encodeURIComponent(runId)}/cancel`,\r\n    { method: \"POST\" },\r\n  );\r\n  return asJsonOrThrow<RunStatusResponse>(response);\r\n}\r\n\r\n// I-ui-003 (#542): report-scoped follow-up. Mirrors FollowUpAnswer in\r\n// src/polaris_v6/followup/schema.py. POST /runs/{id}/followup is real-run\r\n// wired (#680): 422 for aborted/release-blocked, 404 for unknown id.\r\nexport type FollowUpStatus =\r\n  | \"answered\"\r\n  | \"out_of_scope\"\r\n  | \"needs_new_run\"\r\n  | \"evidence_insufficient\";\r\n\r\nexport interface FollowUpAnswer {\r\n  parent_run_id: string;\r\n  question: string;\r\n  status: FollowUpStatus;\r\n  answer_text: string | null;\r\n  used_evidence_ids: string[];\r\n  provenance_tokens: string[];\r\n  rationale: string;\r\n}\r\n\r\nexport async function askFollowup(\r\n  runId: string,\r\n  question: string,\r\n): Promise<FollowUpAnswer> {\r\n  const response = await authFetch(\r\n    `${BACKEND_URL}/runs/${encodeURIComponent(runId)}/followup`,\r\n    {\r\n      method: \"POST\",\r\n      headers: { \"content-type\": \"application/json\" },\r\n      body: JSON.stringify({ question }),\r\n    },\r\n  );\r\n  return asJsonOrThrow<FollowUpAnswer>(response);\r\n}\r\n\r\n// I-ui-004 (#543): two-run compare. Mirrors ReportComparison in\r\n// src/polaris_v6/compare/differ.py. GET /runs/{l}/compare/{r}: 400 if same\r\n// run, 422 abort/release-blocked, 404 unknown (real-run wired #680).\r\nexport interface ReportComparison {\r\n  left_run_id: string;\r\n  right_run_id: string;\r\n  same_template: boolean;\r\n  same_question: boolean;\r\n  shared_evidence_ids: string[];\r\n  only_left_evidence_ids: string[];\r\n  only_right_evidence_ids: string[];\r\n  shared_evidence_pct: number;\r\n  frame_coverage_overlap: string[];\r\n  only_left_frames: string[];\r\n  only_right_frames: string[];\r\n  left_contradictions: number;\r\n  right_contradictions: number;\r\n  pipeline_status_match: boolean;\r\n  family_segregation_both_pass: boolean;\r\n}\r\n\r\nexport async function listCompletedRuns(\r\n  limit = 20,\r\n): Promise<RunStatusResponse[]> {\r\n  const response = await authFetch(\r\n    `${BACKEND_URL}/runs?status=completed&limit=${limit}`,\r\n  );\r\n  return asJsonOrThrow<RunStatusResponse[]>(response);\r\n}\r\n\r\nexport async function compareRuns(\r\n  left: string,\r\n  right: string,\r\n): Promise<ReportComparison> {\r\n  const response = await authFetch(\r\n    `${BACKEND_URL}/runs/${encodeURIComponent(left)}/compare/${encodeURIComponent(right)}`,\r\n  );\r\n  return asJsonOrThrow<ReportComparison>(response);\r\n}\r\n\r\n// I-cd-017 (#627): pin-replay pass_rate: number;\r\n  retracted_source_ids?: string[] | null;\r\n}\r\n\r\nexport async function fetchPinList(runId: string): Promise<PinSnapshot[]> {\r\n  const response = await authFetch(\r\n URL.revokeObjectURL(url);\r\n}\r\n\r\nexport interface StreamEvent {\r\n  event: string;\r\n  data: Record<string, unknown>;\r\n}\r\n\r\n/**\r\n * Subscribe to /stream/{runId}. Returns the EventSource so the caller can\r\n * close it. Per Phase 0 stub, the backend yields 5 deterministic events.\r\n */\r\nexport function subscribeToRun(\r\n  runId: string,\r\n  onEvent: (event: StreamEvent) => void,\r\n  onError?: (error: Event) => void,\r\n): EventSource {\r\n  const source = new EventSource(streamUrl(runId));\r\n\r\n  const handler = (event: MessageEvent<string>) => {\r\n    try {\r\n      const parsed = JSON.parse(event.data);\r\n      onEvent({ event: event.type, data: parsed });\r\n    } catch {\r\n      onEvent({ event: event.type, data: { raw: event.data } });\r\n    }\r\n  };\r\n\r\n  for (const eventName of [\r\n    \"scope_decision\",\r\n    \"retrieval_progress\",\r\n    // I-ui-002 (#707): evidence_id drives the retrieval source feed +\r\n    // sources-read counter; it was previously emitted by the backend but\r\n    // never listened for here, so those SSE events were silently dropped.\r\n    \"evidence_id\",\r\n    \"verifier_verdict\",\r\n    \"section_complete\",\r\n    \"run_complete\",\r\n  ]) {\r\n    source.addEventListener(eventName, handler);\r\n  }\r\n\r\n  if (onError) {\r\n    source.onerror = onError;\r\n  }\r\n  return source;\r\n}\r\n\r\n// null;\r\n}\r\n\r\nexport class RetrievalBadRequestError extends Error {\r\n  code: RetrievalErrorBody[\"code\"];\r\n  decision_id: string | null;\r\n  constructor(body: RetrievalErrorBody) {\r\n runRetrieval(\r\n  decision: IntakeScopeDecision,\r\n): Promise<RetrievalSuccessResponse> {\r\n  const response = await authFetch(`${BACKEND_URL}/api/retrieval`, {\r\n    method: \"POST\",\r\n    headers: { \"content-type\": \"application/json\" },\r\n    body: JSON.stringify({ decision }),\r\n  });\r\n\r\n  if (response.status === 400) {\r\n    const detail = await response.json().catch(() => RetrievalSourceTier,\r\n): number {\r\n  return pool.sources.filter((s) => s.tier === tier).length;\r\n}\r\n\r\n/** Convenience: dedupe + sort sources by tier (T1 first), then by domain. */\r\nexport function sortSourcesByTier(\r\n  sources: RetrievalSource[],\r\n): RetrievalSource[] {\r\n  const tier_rank: Record<RetrievalSourceTier, number> = {\r\n    T1: 0,\r\n    T2: 1,\r\n    T3: 2,\r\n  };\r\n  return [...sources].sort((a, b) => {\r\n    const t = tier_rank[a.tier] - tier_rank[b.tier];\r\n    if (t !== 0) return t;\r\n    return a.domain.localeCompare(b.domain);\r\n  });\r\n}\r\n\r\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\r\n// Slice 003 — Generator with strict-verify\r\n// Mirrors scrollIntoViewIfNeeded(scrollContainer, element, direction, orientation) {\n  if (!scrollContainer || !element || !element.scrollTo) {\n    return;\n  }\n  let targetX = scrollContainer.scrollLeft;\n scrollContainer.scrollHeight;\n  if (isOverflowingX && orientation !== 'vertical') {\n    const elementOffsetLeft = getOffset(scrollContainer, element, 'left');\n    const containerStyles = getStyles(scrollContainer);\n    const elementStyles = getStyles(element);\n    if (direction === 'ltr') {\n      if (elementOffsetLeft + element.offsetWidth + elementStyles.scrollMarginRight > scrollContainer.scrollLeft + scrollContainer.clientWidth - containerStyles.scrollPaddingRight) {\n        // overflow to the right, scroll to align right edges\n        targetX = elementOffsetLeft + element.offsetWidth + elementStyles.scrollMarginRight - scrollContainer.clientWidth + containerStyles.scrollPaddingRight;\n      } else if (elementOffsetLeft - elementStyles.scrollMarginLeft < scrollContainer.scrollLeft + containerStyles.scrollPaddingLeft) {\n        // overflow to the left, scroll to align left edges\n        targetX = elementOffsetLeft - elementStyles.scrollMarginLeft - containerStyles.scrollPaddingLeft;\n      }\n    }\n    if (direction === 'rtl') {\n      if (elementOffsetLeft - elementStyles.scrollMarginRight < scrollContainer.scrollLeft + containerStyles.scrollPaddingLeft) {\n        // overflow to the left, scroll to align left edges\n        targetX = elementOffsetLeft - elementStyles.scrollMarginLeft - containerStyles.scrollPaddingLeft;\n      } else if (elementOffsetLeft + element.offsetWidth + elementStyles.scrollMarginRight > scrollContainer.scrollLeft + scrollContainer.clientWidth - containerStyles.scrollPaddingTop;\n    } else if (elementOffsetTop + element.offsetHeight + elementStyles.scrollMarginBottom > scrollContainer.scrollTop + scrollContainer.clientHeight -     message: \"Please enter a question with at least 3 characters.\",\r\n      });\r\n      return;\r\n    }\r\n    if (looksNonEnglish(trimmed)) {\r\n      setState({\r\n        kind: \"error\",\r\n || file.name.toLowerCase().endsWith(\".pdf\")\r\n  );\r\n}\r\n\r\nexport function PdfDropBanner() {\r\n  const [shown, setShown] = useState(false);\r\n  const [ready, setReady] = useState(false);\r\n\r\n  useEffect(() => {\r\n    const handleDragOver = (e: DragEvent) => e.preventDefault();\r\n    const handleDrop = (e: DragEvent) => {\r\n      const files = e.dataTransfer?.files;\r\n      if (!files || files.length === 0) return;\r\n      let pdfFound = false;\r\n      for (let i = 0; i < files.length; i++) {\r\n        const f = files.item(i);\r\n        if (f && isPdf(f)) {\r\n          pdfFound = true;\r\n          break;\r\n        }\r\n      }\r\n      if (pdfFound) {\r\n        e.preventDefault();\r\n        setShown(true);\r\n      }\r\n    };\r\n    window.addEventListener(\"dragover\", handleDragOver);\r\n    window.addEventListener(\"drop\", handleDrop);\r\n    queueMicrotask(() => setReady(true));\r\n    return () => {\r\n      window.removeEventListener(\"dragover\", handleDragOver);\r\n      window.removeEventListener(\"drop\", handleDrop);\r\n    };\r\n  }, []);\r\n\r\n  if (!shown)\r\n    return (\r\n      <span\r\n        data-testid=\"pdf-drop-ready\"\r\n        data-ready={ready ? \"1\" : \"0\"}\r\n        className=\"sr-only\"\r\n      />\r\n    );\r\n\r\n  return (\r\n    <div\r\n      data-testid=\"pdf-drop-banner\"\r\n      className=\"flex items-center justify-between gap-2 rounded-lg border border-amber-500/40 bg-amber-500/10 p-3 text-sm text-amber-900 dark:text-amber-200\"\r\n    >\r\n      <span>PDFs go through the upload flow (coming soon).</span>\r\n      <button\r\n        type=\"button\"\r\n        data-testid=\"pdf-drop-dismiss\"\r\n        onClick={() => setShown(false)}\r\n        className=\"text-xs font-medium underline-offset-2 hover:underline\"\r\n      >\r\n        Dismiss\r\n      </button>\r\n    </div>\r\n  );\r\n}\r\n"],"names":["BACKEND_URL","authFetch","url","init","headers","Headers","key","value","Object","entries","set","response","globalThis","fetch","status","streamUrl","runId","token","Authorization","replace","base","encodeURIComponent","asJsonOrThrow","body","json","catch","ok","error","Error","ConcurrentRunError","activeRunId","activeStatus","message","name","createRun","payload","method","JSON","stringify","detail","code","active_run_id","active_status","getRun","cancelRun","askFollowup","question","listCompletedRuns","limit","compareRuns","left","right","fetchPinList","fetchPin","date","getUpload","document_id","uploadDocument","file","classification","form","FormData","append","checkAmbiguity","candidates","min_cluster_size","similarity_threshold","checkScope","template","listTemplates","getBundle","getChart","chartType","downloadBundleAsJson","bundle","blob","Blob","type","URL","createObjectURL","link","document","createElement","href","download","run_id","appendChild","click","removeChild","revokeObjectURL","subscribeToRun","onEvent","onError","source","EventSource","handler","event","parsed","parse","data","raw","eventName","addEventListener","onerror","runDisambiguation","IntakeBadRequestError","runIntake","getIntakeHealth","RetrievalBadRequestError","decision_id","runRetrieval","decision","getRetrievalHealth","countSourcesByTier","pool","tier","sources","filter","s","length","sortSourcesByTier","tier_rank","T1","T2","T3","sort","a","b","t","domain","localeCompare","GenerationBadRequestError","pool_id","runGeneration","scope_class","getGenerationHealth","keptSections","report","sections","section_status","keptSentences","section","verified_sentences","verifier_pass","AuditBundleError","report_id","downloadAuditBundle","previewAuditBundle","getAuditBundleHealth","BENCHMARK_DIMENSION_LABELS","sourcing_tier_mix","numeric_grounding","provenance_density","refusal_correctness","coverage_completeness","latency","auditability","ALL_BENCHMARK_DIMENSIONS","get guarantee a positive integer return value, so we can't have\n * a monomorphic `uint` type with `0` meaning empty.\n * See warning note at:\n * https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/Window/requestAnimationFrame#return_value */\nconst EMPTY = null;\nlet LAST_RAF = globalThis.requestAnimationFrame;\nclass Scheduler {\n  /* This implementation uses an array as a backing data-structure for frame callbacks.\n   * It allows `O(1)` callback cancelling by inserting a `null` in the array, though it\n   * never calls the native `cancelAnimationFrame` if there are no frames left. This can\n   * be much more efficient if there is a call pattern that alterns as\n   * \"request-cancel-request-cancel-…\".\n   * But in the case of \"request-request-…-cancel-cancel-…\", it leaves the final animation\n   * frame to run anyway. We turn that frame into a `O(1)` no-op via `callbacksCount`. */\n\n  callbacks = [];\n  callbacksCount = 0;\n  nextId = 1;\n  startId = 1;\n  isScheduled = false;\n  tick = timestamp => {\n    this.isScheduled = false;\n    const currentCallbacks = this.callbacks;\n    const currentCallbacksCount = this.callbacksCount;\n\n    // Update these before iterating, callbacks could call `requestAnimationFrame` again.\n    this.callbacks = [];\n    this.callbacksCount = 0;\n    this.startId = this.nextId;\n    if (currentCallbacksCount > 0) {\n      for (let i = 0; i < currentCallbacks.length; i += 1) {\n        currentCallbacks[i]?.(timestamp);\n      }\n    }\n  };\n  request(fn) {\n    const id = this.nextId;\n    this.nextId += 1;\n    this.callbacks.push(fn);\n    this.callbacksCount += 1;\n\n    /* In a test environment with fake timers, a fake `requestAnimationFrame` can be called\n     * but there's no guarantee that the animation frame will actually run before the fake\n     * timers are teared, which leaves `isScheduled` set, but won't run our `tick()`. */\n    const didRAFChange = process.env.NODE_ENV !== 'production' && LAST_RAF !== requestAnimationFrame && (LAST_RAF = requestAnimationFrame, true);\n    if (!this.isScheduled || didRAFChange) {\n      requestAnimationFrame(this.tick);\n      this.isScheduled = true;\n    }\n    return id;\n  }\n  cancel(id) {\n    const index = id - this.startId;\n    if (index < 0 || index >= this.callbacks.length) {\n      return;\n    }\n    this.callbacks[index] = null;\n    this.callbacksCount -= 1;\n  }\n}\nconst scheduler = new Scheduler();\nexport class AnimationFrame {\n  static create() {\n    return new AnimationFrame();\n  }\n  static request(fn) {\n    return scheduler.request(fn);\n  }\n  static cancel(id) {\n    return scheduler.cancel(id);\n  }\n  currentId = EMPTY;\n\n  /**\n   * Executes `fn` after `delay`, clearing any previously scheduled call.\n   */\n  request(fn) {\n    this.cancel();\n    this.currentId = scheduler.request(() => {\n      this.currentId = EMPTY;\n      fn();\n    });\n  }\n  cancel = () => {\n    if (this.currentId !== EMPTY) {\n      scheduler.cancel(this.currentId);\n      this.currentId = EMPTY;\n    }\n  };\n  disposeEffect = () => {\n    return this.cancel;\n  };\n}\n\n/**\n * A `requestAnimationFrame` with automatic cleanup and guard.\n */\nexport function useAnimationFrame() {\n  const timeout = useRefWithInit(AnimationFrame.create).current;\n  useOnMount(timeout.disposeEffect);\n  return timeout;\n}","/**\n * ]);\r\n  const [claims, set_claims] = useState<ExpectedClaim[]>([\r\n    {\r\n      claim_id: \"c1\",\r\n      statement: \"\",\r\n      expected_entities: [],\r\n      required_jurisdictions: URL.revokeObjectURL(url);\r\n    }\r\n  }\r\n\r\n  function toggle_jur(j: Jurisdiction, on: boolean) {\r\n    set_jurs(on ?     <FieldLabel>Research question</FieldLabel>\r\n            <Input\r\n              data-testid=\"ce-question\"\r\n              value={research_question}\r\n              onChange={(e) => <label\r\n                    key={j}\r\n                    className={`focus-within:ring-ring/70 cursor-pointer rounded-full border px-3 py-1 text-xs font-medium transition-colors \"outcome\",\r\n];\r\n\r\nexport interface ExpectedEntity {\r\n  name: string;\r\n  aliases: string[];\r\n  entity_type: EntityType;\r\n}\r\nexport interface ExpectedClaim {\r\n  claim_id: string;\r\n Math.random().toString(36).slice(2)\r\n  );\r\n}\r\n\r\nexport function buildContract(\r\n  args: Omit<\r\n    EvidenceContract,\r\n    \"contract_id\" | \"contract_version\" | \"created_at_utc\"\r\n  >,\r\n): EvidenceContract {\r\n  return {\r\n    contract_id: uuid(),\r\n    contract_version: \"1.0\",\r\n    created_at_utc: new Date().toISOString(),\r\n    ...args,\r\n  };\r\n}\r\n\r\nexport function validateContract(c: EvidenceContract): string[] {\r\n  const errs: string[] = [];\r\n  if (!c.research_question.trim()) errs.push(\"research_question required\");\r\n  if (!c.created_by.trim()) errs.push(\"created_by required\");\r\n  if (c.expected_entities.length === 0) errs.push(\"at least 1 entity required\");\r\n  if (c.expected_claims.length === 0) errs.push(\"at least 1 claim required\");\r\n  if (c.jurisdictions.length === 0)\r\n    errs.push(\"at least 1 jurisdiction required\");\r\n  for (const e of c.expected_entities) {\r\n    if (!e.name.trim()) errs.push(\"entity name required\");\r\n  }\r\n  const cov = c.expected_source_coverage;\r\n  for (const [k, v] of Object.entries(cov)) {\r\n    if (v < 0 || v > 100) errs.push(`${k} must be in [0, 100]`);\r\n  }\r\n  const names = c.expected_entities.map((e) => e.name);\r\n  if (new Set(names).size !== names.length)\r\n    errs.push(\"duplicate entity name(s)\");\r\n  const ids = c.expected_claims.map((cl) => cl.claim_id);\r\n  if (new Set(ids).size !== ids.length) errs.push(\"duplicate claim_id(s)\");\r\n  const declared = new Set(names);\r\n  const cjurs = new Set(c.jurisdictions);\r\n  for (const cl of c.expected_claims) {\r\n    if (!cl.claim_id.trim()) errs.push(\"claim_id required\");\r\n    if (!cl.statement.trim())\r\n      errs.push(`claim ${cl.claim_id} statement required`);\r\n    if (cl.expected_entities.length === 0)\r\n      errs.push(`claim ${cl.claim_id} expected_entities required`);\r\n    if (cl.required_jurisdictions.length === 0)\r\n      errs.push(`claim ${cl.claim_id} required_jurisdictions required`);\r\n    for (const e of cl.expected_entities) {\r\n      if (!declared.has(e))\r\n        errs.push(`claim ${cl.claim_id} references undeclared entity ${e}`);\r\n    }\r\n    for (const j of cl.required_jurisdictions) {\r\n      if (!cjurs.has(j))\r\n        errs.push(\r\n          `claim ${cl.claim_id} jurisdiction ${j} not in contract jurisdictions`,\r\n        );\r\n    }\r\n  }\r\n  return === 'ltr') {\n      if (elementOffsetLeft + element.offsetWidth + elementStyles.scrollMarginRight > scrollContainer.scrollLeft + scrollContainer.clientWidth - containerStyles.scrollPaddingRight) {\n        // overflow to the right, scroll to align right edges\n        targetX = elementOffsetLeft + element.offsetWidth + elementStyles.scrollMarginRight - scrollContainer.clientWidth + containerStyles.scrollPaddingRight;\n      } else if (elementOffsetLeft - elementStyles.scrollMarginLeft < scrollContainer.scrollLeft + containerStyles.scrollPaddingLeft) {\n        // overflow to the left, scroll to align left edges\n        targetX = elementOffsetLeft - elementStyles.scrollMarginLeft - containerStyles.scrollPaddingLeft;\n      }\n    }\n    if (direction === 'rtl') {\n      if (elementOffsetLeft - elementStyles.scrollMarginRight < scrollContainer.scrollLeft + containerStyles.scrollPaddingLeft) {\n        // overflow to the left, scroll to align left edges\n        targetX = elementOffsetLeft - elementStyles.scrollMarginLeft - containerStyles.scrollPaddingLeft;\n      } else if (elementOffsetLeft + element.offsetWidth + elementStyles.scrollMarginRight > scrollContainer.scrollLeft + scrollContainer.clientWidth - containerStyles.scrollPaddingRight) {\n        // overflow to the right, scroll to align right edges\n        targetX = elementOffsetLeft + element.offsetWidth + elementStyles.scrollMarginRight - scrollContainer.clientWidth + containerStyles.scrollPaddingRight;\n      }\n    }\n  }\n  if (isOverflowingY && orientation !== 'horizontal') elementStyles.scrollMarginTop - containerStyles.scrollPaddingTop;\n    } else if (elementOffsetTop + element.offsetHeight + elementStyles.scrollMarginBottom > scrollContainer.scrollTop + scrollContainer.clientHeight - containerStyles.scrollPaddingBottom) {\n      // overflow downwards, align bottom edges\n      targetY = elementOffsetTop + element.offsetHeight + elementStyles.scrollMarginBottom - scrollContainer.clientHeight + containerStyles.scrollPaddingBottom;\n    }\n  }\n  scrollContainer.scrollTo({\n    left: targetX,\n    top: targetY,\n    behavior: 'offsetLeft' : 'offsetTop';\n  let result = 0;\n  while (element.offsetParent) {\n    result += element[propName];\n    if (element.offsetParent === ancestor) {\n      break;\n    }\n    element = .next\server\chunks\ssr\_0d_viee._.js.map:1:{"version":3,"sources":["../../../../lib/api.ts","../../../../components/ui/card.tsx","../../../../app/benchmark/components/benchmark_board.tsx"],"sourcesContent":["/**\r\n * POLARIS v6 API client.\r\n *\r\n * Mirrors the FastAPI contract in src/polaris_v6/api/{runs,health,stream}.py.\r\n *\r\n * I-carney-005 (2026-05-13): all requests are browser-relative paths so the\r\n * browser only ever talks to the webui origin. Next.js server-side rewrites\r\n * (web/next.config.ts) forward `/api/v6/*` → `${INTERNAL_API_URL}/*`, which\r\n * in production compose is `http://api:8000` (a Docker service name the\r\n * browser cannot resolve directly). In local dev, INTERNAL_API_URL defaults\r\n * to http://localhost:8000 so `npm run dev` against a host-port-mapped\r\n * backend works without any client-side configuration.\r\n *\r\n * Codex diff iter-1 P1-B: NEXT_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL is no longer consulted at\r\n * runtime — baking an absolute http://api:8000 into the client bundle would\r\n * make production browsers attempt direct Docker-network reach and fail.\r\n */\r\n\r\nimport { authHeader, redirectToSignIn } from \"@/lib/auth\";\r\n\r\nconst BACKEND_URL = \"/api/v6\";\r\n\r\n/**\r\n * I-rdy-004 (#500) — fetch wrapper for every v6 API call. Injects the\r\n * `Authorization: Bearer <jwt>` header, preserves any caller-supplied\r\n * headers, and does NOT force a content-type (so `FormData` uploads keep\r\n * their auto-generated multipart boundary). On a 401 it clears the token\r\n * and routes to /sign-in.\r\n */\r\nasync function authFetch(\r\n  url: string,\r\n  init: RequestInit = {},\r\n): Promise<Response> {\r\n  const headers = new Headers(init.headers ?? {});\r\n  for (const [key, value] of Object.entries(authHeader())) {\r\n    headers.set(key, value);\r\n  }\r\n  const response = await globalThis.fetch(url, { ...init, headers });\r\n  if (response.status === 401) {\r\n    redirectToSignIn();\r\n  }\r\n  return response;\r\n}\r\n\r\n/**\r\n * I-rdy-004 — SSE URL for a run's event stream. Native `EventSource` cannot\r\n * set request headers, so the JWT rides as the `access_token` query param;\r\n * the backend `require_auth` accepts it for `/stream/*` paths only.\r\n */\r\nexport function streamUrl(runId: string): string {\r\n  const token = authHeader().Authorization?.replace(/^Bearer /, \"\");\r\n  const base = `${BACKEND_URL}/stream/${encodeURIComponent(runId)}`;\r\n  return token ? `${base}?access_token=${encodeURIComponent(token)}` : base;\r\n}\r\n\r\nexport type TemplateId =\r\n  | \"clinical\"\r\n  | \"policy\"\r\n  | \"tech\"\r\n  | \"due_diligence\"\r\n  | \"ai_sovereignty\"\r\n  | \"canada_us\"\r\n  | \"workforce\"\r\n  | \"custom\";\r\n\r\nexport type RunStatus =\r\n  | \"queued\"\r\n  | \"in_progress\"\r\n  | \"completed\"\r\n  | \"cancelled\"\r\n  | \"failed\"\r\n  | \"abort_scope_rejected\"\r\n  | \"abort_corpus_inadequate\"\r\n  | \"abort_corpus_approval_denied\"\r\n  | \"abort_no_verified_sections\";\r\n\r\nexport interface RunRequest {\r\n  template: TemplateId;\r\n  question: string;\r\n  document_ids?: string[];\r\n}\r\n\r\nexport interface RunStatusResponse {\r\n  run_id: string;\r\n  status: RunStatus;\r\n  // I-ui-bug-001 (#725): the pipeline-A verdict (success / abort_* / partial_*\r\n  // / error_*), distinct from the lifecycle `status`. mark_aborted() stores\r\n  // lifecycle_status='completed' for aborts, so lifecycle alone CANNOT tell a\r\n  // successful run from an aborted one — pipeline_status is required to render\r\n  // a completed run honestly (an aborted run must NOT show as success).\r\n  pipeline_status?: string | null;\r\n  // I-rdy-011 (#507): true once a cancel has been requested; lets the run\r\n  // page show a cancel as in-flight before an in-progress run transitions.\r\n  cancel_requested?: boolean;\r\n  template: string;\r\n  question: string;\r\n  queued_at: string;\r\n  started_at?: string | null;\r\n  finished_at?: string | null;\r\n}\r\n\r\nexport interface ApiError extends Error {\r\n  status: number;\r\n  body: unknown;\r\n}\r\n\r\nasync function asJsonOrThrow<T>(response: Response): Promise<T> {\r\n  const body = await response.json().catch(() => null);\r\n  if (!response.ok) {\r\n    const error = new Error(\r\n      `POLARIS backend returned ${response.status}`,\r\n    ) as ApiError;\r\n    error.status = response.status;\r\n    error.body = body;\r\n    throw error;\r\n  }\r\n  return body as T;\r\n}\r\n\r\n/**\r\n * Thrown by createRun when POST /runs is rejected by the 1-concurrent-session\r\n * constraint (I-rdy-013): a research run is already queued or in progress.\r\n */\r\nexport class ConcurrentRunError extends Error {\r\n  readonly activeRunId: string;\r\n  readonly activeStatus: string;\r\n  constructor(message: string, activeRunId: string, activeStatus: string) {\r\n    super(message);\r\n    this.name = \"ConcurrentRunError\";\r\n    this.activeRunId = activeRunId;\r\n    this.activeStatus = activeStatus;\r\n  }\r\n}\r\n\r\nexport async function createRun(\r\n  payload: RunRequest,\r\n): Promise<RunStatusResponse> {\r\n  const response = await authFetch(`${BACKEND_URL}/runs`, {\r\n    method: \"POST\",\r\n    headers: { \"content-type\": \"application/json\" },\r\n    body: JSON.stringify(payload),\r\n  });\r\n  if (response.status === 409) {\r\n    // Read the body exactly once, then unwrap FastAPI's { detail: {...} }\r\n    // envelope. The 1-concurrent-session reject carries a structured detail;\r\n    // any other 409 falls through to a generic ApiError.\r\n    const body = (await response.json().catch(() => null)) as {\r\n      detail?: unknown;\r\n    } | null;\r\n    const detail = (body?.detail ?? body) as {\r\n      code?: string;\r\n      message?: string;\r\n      active_run_id?: string;\r\n      active_status?: string;\r\n    } | null;\r\n    if (detail && detail.code === \"concurrent_run_active\") {\r\n      throw new ConcurrentRunError(\r\n        detail.message ?? \"A research run is already in progress.\",\r\n        detail.active_run_id ?? \"\",\r\n        detail.active_status ?? \"in_progress\",\r\n      );\r\n    }\r\n    const error = new Error(\"POLARIS backend returned 409\") as ApiError;\r\n    error.status = 409;\r\n    error.body = body;\r\n    throw error;\r\n  }\r\n  return asJsonOrThrow<RunStatusResponse>(response);\r\n}\r\n\r\nexport async function getRun(runId: string): Promise<RunStatusResponse> {\r\n  const response = await authFetch(`${BACKEND_URL}/runs/${runId}`);\r\n  return asJsonOrThrow<RunStatusResponse>(response);\r\n}\r\n\r\n// I-rdy-011 (#507): request cancellation of a run. void,\r\n): EventSource {\r\n  const source = new EventSource(streamUrl(runId));\r\n\r\n  const handler = (event: MessageEvent<string>) => {\r\n    try {\r\n      const parsed = JSON.parse(event.data);\r\n      onEvent({ event: event.type, data: parsed });\r\n    } catch {\r\n      onEvent({ event: event.type, data: { raw: event.data } });\r\n    }\r\n  };\r\n\r\n  for (const eventName of [\r\n    \"scope_decision\",\r\n    \"retrieval_progress\",\r\n    // I-ui-002 (#707): evidence_id drives the retrieval source feed +\r\n    // sources-read counter; it was previously emitted by the backend but\r\n    // never listened for here, so those SSE events were silently dropped.\r\n    \"evidence_id\",\r\n    \"verifier_verdict\",\r\n    \"section_complete\",\r\n    \"run_complete\",\r\n  ]) {\r\n    source.addEventListener(eventName, handler);\r\n  }\r\n\r\n  if (onError) {\r\n    source.onerror = onError;\r\n  }\r\n  return source;\r\n}\r\n\r\n// function sortSourcesByTier(\r\n  sources: RetrievalSource[],\r\n): RetrievalSource[] {\r\n  const tier_rank: Record<RetrievalSourceTier, number> = {\r\n    T1: 0,\r\n    T2: 1,\r\n    T3: 2,\r\n  };\r\n  return [...sources].sort((a, b) => {\r\n    const t = tier_rank[a.tier] - tier_rank[b.tier];\r\n    if (t !== 0) return t;\r\n    return a.domain.localeCompare(b.domain);\r\n  });\r\n}\r\n\r\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\r\n// Slice 003 — Generator with strict-verify\r\n// Mirrors return `${Math.round(v * 100)}%`;\r\n}\r\n\r\nfunction tone_class(score: number | null, peers: (number | null)[]): string {\r\n  if (score === null) return \"text-muted-foreground italic\";\r\n  const populated = peers.filter((p): p is number => p !== null);\r\n  if (populated.length === 0) return \"\";\r\n  const max_peer = Math.max(...populated);\r\n  if (score > max_peer) {\r\n    return \"text-emerald-700 dark:text-emerald-300 font-semibold\";\r\n  }\r\n  if (score < max_peer) return \"text-muted-foreground\";\r\n  return \"\";\r\n}\r\n\r\nexport function BenchmarkBoard() {\r\n  const [state, setState] = useState<BoardState>({ kind: \"loading_health\" });\r\n\r\n  useEffect(() => {\r\n    let cancelled = false;\r\n    (async () => {\r\n      try {\r\n        const health =       if (health.available_benchmarks.length === 0) {\r\n          setState({\r\n            kind: \"no_benchmarks\",\r\n            results_root: health.results_root,\r\n          });\r\n          return;\r\n        }\r\n        setState({\r\n          kind: \"benchmark_list\",\r\n          available: health.available_benchmarks,\r\n        });\r\n      } catch (err) {\r\n        if (cancelled) err.message : \"Unknown error\";\r\n      setState({ kind: \"error\", message: msg });\r\n    }\r\n  }\r\n\r\n  if (state.kind === \"loading_health\") {\r\n    return (\r\n      <Card }\r\n\r\n  if (state.kind === \"error\") {\r\n    return (\r\n      <Card\r\n        data-testid=\"benchmark-error\"\r\n        className=\"border-rose-500/40 bg-rose-500/5\"\r\n      >\r\n {ALL_BENCHMARK_DIMENSIONS.length} dimensions\r\n          </span>\r\n        </CardHeader>\r\n        <CardContent>\r\n          <p className=\"text-sm\" data-testid=\"benchmark-tally\">\r\n https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/Window/requestAnimationFrame#return_value */\nconst EMPTY = null;\nlet LAST_RAF = globalThis.requestAnimationFrame;\nclass Scheduler {\n  /* This implementation uses an array as a backing data-structure for frame callbacks.\n   * It allows `O(1)` callback cancelling by inserting a `null` in the array, though it\n   * never calls the native `cancelAnimationFrame` if there are no frames left. This can\n   * be much more efficient if there is a call pattern that alterns as\n   * \"request-cancel-request-cancel-…\".\n   * But in the case of \"request-request-…-cancel-cancel-…\", it leaves the final animation\n   * frame to run anyway. We turn that frame into a `O(1)` no-op via `callbacksCount`. */\n\n  callbacks = [];\n  callbacksCount = 0;\n  nextId = 1;\n  startId = 1;\n  isScheduled = false;\n  tick = timestamp => {\n    this.isScheduled = false;\n    const currentCallbacks = this.callbacks;\n    const currentCallbacksCount = this.callbacksCount;\n\n    // Update these before iterating, callbacks could call `requestAnimationFrame` again.\n    this.callbacks = [];\n    this.callbacksCount = 0;\n    this.startId = this.nextId;\n    if (currentCallbacksCount > 0) {\n      for (let i = 0; i < currentCallbacks.length; i += 1) {\n        currentCallbacks[i]?.(timestamp);\n      }\n    }\n  };\n  request(fn) {\n    const id = this.nextId;\n    this.nextId += 1;\n    this.callbacks.push(fn);\n    this.callbacksCount += 1;\n\n    /* In a test environment with fake timers, a fake `requestAnimationFrame` can be called\n     * but there's no guarantee that the animation frame will actually run before the fake\n     * timers are teared, which leaves `isScheduled` set, but won't run our `tick()`. */\n    const didRAFChange = process.env.NODE_ENV !== 'production' && LAST_RAF !== requestAnimationFrame && (LAST_RAF = requestAnimationFrame, true);\n    if (!this.isScheduled || didRAFChange) {\n      requestAnimationFrame(this.tick);\n      this.isScheduled = true;\n    }\n    return id;\n  }\n  cancel(id) {\n    const index = id - this.startId;\n    if (index < 0 || index >= this.callbacks.length) {\n      return;\n    }\n    this.callbacks[index] = null;\n    this.callbacksCount -= 1;\n  }\n}\nconst scheduler = new Scheduler();\nexport class AnimationFrame {\n  static create() {\n    return new AnimationFrame();\n  }\n  static request(fn) {\n    return scheduler.request(fn);\n  }\n  static cancel(id) {\n    return scheduler.cancel(id);\n  }\n  currentId = EMPTY;\n\n  /**\n   * Executes `fn` after `delay`, clearing any previously scheduled call.\n   */\n  request(fn) {\n    this.cancel();\n    this.currentId = scheduler.request(() => {\n      this.currentId = EMPTY;\n      fn();\n    });\n  }\n  cancel = () => {\n    if (this.currentId !== EMPTY) {\n      scheduler.cancel(this.currentId);\n      this.currentId = EMPTY;\n    }\n  };\n  disposeEffect = () => {\n    return this.cancel;\n  };\n}\n\n/**\n * A `requestAnimationFrame` with automatic
